These institutionalized barriers deny young people's right to bodily autonomy and can force young people to encounter an abusive parent or guardian, ignores trusted relationships young people may have with adults other than a parent or legal guardian, and in the case of the judicial bypass process, may force young BIPOC ORG to interact with a legal system that has historically targeted and caused harm to communities of color.(16) These and other government-created and government-institutionalized barriers--including the restriction on funding for abortion services in Federal programs ORG --exacerbate and create poverty and racial inequality in income, wealth-generation, and access to services.(17) Access to health care, including abortion services, promotes the general welfare of people living in the United States GPE . Singling out abortion services for funding restrictions in health care programs otherwise designed to promote the health and well-being of people in the United States GPE has cost pregnant people their lives, their livelihoods, their ability to obtain or maintain economic security for themselves and their families, their ability to meet their family's basic needs, their ability to continue their education without disruption, and their ability to break free of abusive relationships.(18) Like other health care and health insurance markets in the United States GPE , abortion services and public insurance programs are commercial activities that affect interstate commerce. Providers and patients travel across State ORG lines, and otherwise engage in interstate commerce, to provide and access abortion services. Material goods, services, and federally regulated medications used in abortion services circulate in interstate commerce.(19) Congress ORG has the authority to enact this Act to ensure affordable coverage of abortion and other services pursuant to--(A) its powers under the necessary and proper clause of section 8 LAW , article I of the Constitution of the United States;(B) its powers under the commerce clause of section 8 LAW , article 1 of the Constitution of the United States;(C LAW ) its powers to tax and spend for the general welfare under section 8 LAW , article 1 of the Constitution of the United States LAW ; and(D) its powers to enforce section 1 of the Fourteenth Amendment LAW under section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment LAW to the Constitution of the United States.(20) Congress ORG has exercised these constitutional powers to create, expand, and insure health care access for people in the United States GPE for decades DATE . Pursuant to this constitutional authority, Congress ORG has enacted, and subsequently reauthorized, numerous health care programs including but not limited to title XVIII of the Social Security Act of 1965 DATE ( Medicare ORG ); title XIX of the Social Security Act of 1965 DATE ( Medicaid ORG ); and title XXI of the Social Security Act (Children's Health Insurance Program ORG , enacted in 1997).SEC DATE . 3 CARDINAL . DEFINITIONS.For GPE purposes of this Act:(1) Abortion services.--The term ``abortion services'' means an abortion and any services related to and provided in conjunction with an abortion, whether or not provided at the same time or on the same day DATE as the abortion.(2) Health program or plan.--The term ``health program or plan'' means the following health programs or plans that pay the cost of, or provide, health care:(A) The Medicaid ORG program under title XIX of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. DATE 1396 DATE et seq.).(B) The Children's Health Insurance Program ORG under title XXI of the Social Security Act ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1397 DATE et seq.).(C) The Medicare ORG program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1395 DATE et seq.).(D) A Medicare ORG supplemental policy as defined in section 1882(g)(1 LAW ) of the Social Security Act ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1395ss(g)(1)).(E CARDINAL ) The Indian Health Service ORG program under the Indian Health Care Improvement Act ORG ( 25 U.S.C. DATE 1601 DATE et seq.).(F) Medical care and health benefits under the TRICARE ORG program ( 10 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1071 et seq.).(G) Benefits for veterans under chapter 17 LAW of title 38, United States ORG Code, and medical care for survivors and dependents of veterans ( 38 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1781 DATE et seq.).(H) Benefits under the uniform health benefits program for employees of the Department of Defense ORG assigned to a nonappropriated fund instrumentality of the Department ORG established under section 349 LAW of the National Defense Authorization Act ORG for Fiscal Year 1995 DATE (Public Law 103 CARDINAL -337; 10 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1587 note).(I) Medical care for individuals in the care or custody of the Department of Homeland Security ORG pursuant to any of sections 235 CARDINAL , 236 CARDINAL , or 241 CARDINAL of the Immigration and Nationality Act ORG ( 8 U.S.C. 1225, 1226 DATE , 1231).(J) Medical care for individuals in the care or custody of the Department of Health and Human Services ORG , Office of Refugee Resettlement ORG under section 235 LAW of the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act FAC of 2008 ( 8 U.S.C. 1232 DATE ) or section 462 CARDINAL of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 ( 6 CARDINAL U.S.C. 279).(K) Medical assistance to refugees under section 412 LAW of the Immigration and Nationality Act ORG ( 8 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1522).(L) Other coverage, such as a State ORG health benefits risk pool, as the Secretary of Health and Human Services ORG , in coordination with the Secretary of the Treasury ORG , recognizes for purposes of section 5000A(f)(1)(E) of the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE ( 26 CARDINAL U.S.C. 5000A(f)(1)(E)).(M) The Federal Employees Health Benefit Plan ORG under chapter 89 LAW of title 5, United States GPE Code.(N) Medical care for individuals under the care or custody of the Department of Justice ORG pursuant to chapter 301 LAW of title 18 (18 U.S.C. 4001 DATE et seq.).(O) Medical care for Peace Corps ORG volunteers under section 5(e LAW ) of the Peace Corps Act ORG ( 22 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2504(e)).(P CARDINAL ) Other government-sponsored programs established after the date of the enactment of this Act. SEC ORG . 4 CARDINAL . ABORTION COVERAGE AND CARE REGARDLESS OF INCOME OR SOURCE OF INSURANCE.(a) Ensuring Abortion Coverage and Care Through The Federal Government In Its Role ORG as an Insurer GPE and Employer.--Each person insured by, enrolled in, or otherwise receiving medical care from health programs or plans described in section 3(2 LAW ) shall receive coverage of abortion services. Health programs or plans described in section 3(2 LAW ) shall provide coverage of abortion services.(b) Ensuring Abortion Coverage and Care Through The Federal Government In Its Role ORG As a Health Care Provider.--In its role as a provider of health services including in health programs and plans described in section 3(2 LAW ), the Federal Government ORG shall ensure access to abortion services for individuals who are eligible to receive medical care in its own facilities or in facilities with which it contracts to provide medical care.(c) Prohibiting Restrictions on Private Insurance Coverage of Abortion Services.--The Federal Government ORG shall not prohibit, restrict, or otherwise inhibit insurance coverage of abortion services by State ORG or local government or by private health plans. SEC ORG . 5 CARDINAL . REPEAL OF SECTION 1303.(a DATE ) In General.--Section 1303 CARDINAL of the Patient Protection ORG and Affordable Care Act ( 42 U.S.C. 18023 DATE ) is repealed.(b) Conforming Amendments.--(1) Basic health plans.--Section 1331(d) of the Patient Protection ORG and Affordable Care Act ( 42 U.S.C. 18051(d DATE )) is amended by striking paragraph ( 4).(2 WORK_OF_ART ) Multi-state plans.--Section 1334(a) of the Patient Protection ORG and Affordable Care Act (Public Law 111 CARDINAL -148) is amended--(A ORG ) by striking paragraph ( 6 CARDINAL ); and(B ORG ) by redesignating paragraph (7) as paragraph ( 6).SEC CARDINAL . 6 CARDINAL . SENSE OF CONGRESS.It is the sense of Congress ORG that--(1) the Federal Government ORG , acting in its capacity as an insurer, employer, or health care provider, should serve as a model for the Nation to ensure coverage of abortion services; and(2) restrictions on coverage of abortion services in the private insurance market must end. SEC ORG . 7 CARDINAL . RULE OF CONSTRUCTION.Nothing in this Act shall be construed to have any effect on any Federal, State ORG , or local law that includes more protections for abortion coverage or services than those set forth in this Act. SEC ORG . 8 CARDINAL . RELATIONSHIP TO FEDERAL LAW.This Act supersedes and applies to all Federal law, and the implementation of that law, whether statutory or otherwise, and whether adopted before or after the date of enactment of this Act and is not subject to the Religious Freedom Restoration Act EVENT of 1993 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000bb ORG et seq.).SEC. 9. SEVERABILITY.If any portion of this Act or the application thereof to any person, entity, government, or circumstances is held invalid, such invalidity shall not affect the portions or applications of this Act which can be given effect without the invalid portion or application. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. ORG 790 CARDINAL Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 790 To amend the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE to repeal the firearmtransfer tax, and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVESFebruary 2 ORG , 2023 Mrs. Hinson PERSON (for herself, Mr. Crawford PERSON , Mr. Arrington PERSON , Mr. Posey PERSON , Mr. Mann PERSON , Mr. Kelly PERSON of Pennsylvania GPE , Mr. Hudson PERSON , Mrs. Miller-Meeks PERSON , Mr. Johnson PERSON of Ohio GPE , Mr. Mooney PERSON , Mr. Walberg PERSON , Mr. Weber PERSON of Texas GPE , Ms. Tenney PERSON , Mr. Higgins PERSON of Louisiana GPE , Mr. Banks PERSON , Ms. Greene PERSON of Georgia GPE , Mr. Amodei PERSON , and Mr. Womack PERSON ) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means A ORG BILLTo amend the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE to repeal the firearmtransfer tax, and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Repealing Illegal Freedom and Liberty Excises Act'' PRODUCT or the `` RIFLE ORG Act''. SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . REPEAL OF CERTAIN TAXES RELATING TO FIREARMS.(a) Firearm PERSON Transfer Tax Repealed.--Section 5811 DATE of the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE is hereby repealed.(b) Conforming Amendments.--(1) Section 4182(a) of such Code PERSON is amended by striking ``on which the tax provided by section 5811 LAW has been paid'' and inserting ``with respect to which the tax provided by section 5811 LAW (as in effect immediately before the enactment of the Repealing Illegal Freedom and Liberty Excises Act EVENT ) would have applied but for its repeal''.(2) Section 5846 of such Code PERSON is amended by striking ``, 5811,''.(3) Section 5852 of such Code PERSON is amended by striking ``section 5811'' each place it appears and inserting ``section 5811 (as in effect immediately before the enactment of the Repealing Illegal Freedom and Liberty EVENT Excises Act)''.(4) Section 5853 of such Code PERSON is amended by striking ``section 5811'' and inserting ``section 5811 (as in effect immediately before the enactment of the Repealing Illegal Freedom and Liberty EVENT Excises Act)''.(5) Section 5854 of such Code PERSON is amended by striking ``section 5811'' and inserting ``section 5811 (as in effect immediately before the enactment of the Repealing Illegal Freedom and Liberty Excises EVENT Act)''.(c) Effective Date.--The amendments made by this section shall apply to transfers after the date of the enactment of this Act. SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . RULE OF CONSTRUCTION.Nothing in this Act shall be construed to place any firearms regulated under Chapter 53 LAW of the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE ( the National Firearms Act LAW ) under the jurisdiction of the United States GPE Consumer Product Safety Commission ORG . &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. Panetta PERSON , Mr. Pappas PERSON , Mr. Pascrell PERSON , Mr. Payne PERSON , Ms. Pelosi PERSON , Mrs. Peltola PERSON , Mr. Peters PERSON , Ms. Pettersen PERSON , Mr. Phillips PERSON , Ms. Pingree PERSON , Ms. Plaskett PERSON , Mr. Pocan PERSON , Ms. Porter PERSON , Ms. Pressley PERSON , Mr. Quigley PERSON , Mrs. Ramirez PERSON , Mr. Raskin PERSON ,Ms. Ross PERSON , Mr. Ruiz PERSON , Mr. Ruppersberger PERSON , Mr. Ryan PERSON , Mr. Sablan PERSON , Ms. Salinas PERSON , Ms. Sanchez PERSON , Mr. Sarbanes PERSON , Ms. Scanlon PERSON , Ms. Schakowsky PERSON , Mr. Schiff PERSON , Mr. Schneider PERSON , Ms. Scholten PERSON , Ms. Schrier PERSON , Mr. Scott PERSON of Virginia GPE , Mr. David Scott PERSON of Georgia GPE , Ms. Sewell PERSON , Mr. Sherman PERSON , Ms. Sherrill PERSON , Ms. Slotkin PERSON , Mr. Smith PERSON of Washington GPE , Mr. Sorensen PERSON , Mr. Soto PERSON ,Ms. Spanberger PERSON , Ms. Stansbury PERSON , Mr. Stanton PERSON , Ms. Stevens PERSON , Ms. Strickland PERSON , Mr. Swalwell PERSON , Mrs. Sykes PERSON , Mr. Thanedar PERSON , Mr. Thompson PERSON of California GPE , Mr. Thompson PERSON of Mississippi GPE , Ms. Titus PERSON , Ms. Tlaib PERSON , Ms. Tokuda PERSON , Mr. Tonko PERSON , Mr. Torres PERSON of New York GPE , Mrs. Torres PERSON of California GPE , Mrs. Trahan PERSON , Mr. Trone PERSON , Ms. Underwood PERSON , Mr. Vargas PERSON , Mr. Vasquez PERSON , Mr. Veasey PERSON , Ms. Velazquez PERSON , Ms. Wasserman Schultz PERSON , Ms. Waters PERSON , Mrs. Watson Coleman PERSON , Ms. Wexton PERSON , Ms. Wild PERSON , Ms. Williams PERSON of Georgia GPE , and Ms. Wilsonof Florida PERSON ) introduced the following bill; which was referred to theCommittee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committees on Education and the Workforce, Financial Services ORG , House Administration ORG ,and Oversight and Accountability WORK_OF_ART , for a period to be subsequentlydetermined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of suchprovisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned A BILLTo prohibit discrimination on the basis of sex SEX , gender GENDER identity, and sexual orientation SEXUALITY , and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Equality Act'' PRODUCT . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . FINDINGS AND PURPOSE.(a) Findings.--Congress finds the following:(1) Discrimination can occur on the basis of the sex SEX , sexual orientation SEXUALITY , gender GENDER identity, pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition of an individual, as well as because of sex SEX -based stereotypes. Each of these factors alone can serve as the basis for discrimination, and each is a form of sex SEX discrimination.(2) A single instance of discrimination may have more than one CARDINAL basis. For example, discrimination against a married same- sex SEX couple could be based on the sex SEX stereotype that marriage should only be between heterosexual couples, the sexual orientation SEXUALITY of the two CARDINAL individuals in the couple, or both. In addition, some persons are subjected to discrimination based on a combination or the intersection of multiple protected characteristics. Discrimination against a pregnant lesbian could be based on her sex SEX , her sexual orientation SEXUALITY , her pregnancy, or on the basis of multiple factors.(3) Lesbian NORP , gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer SEXUALITY (referred to as `` LGBTQ IDENTITY '') people commonly experience discrimination in securing access to public accommodations--including restaurants, senior centers, stores, places of or establishments that provide entertainment, health care facilities, shelters, government offices, youth service providers including adoption and foster care providers, and transportation. Forms of discrimination include the exclusion and denial of entry, unequal or unfair treatment, harassment, and violence. This discrimination prevents the full participation of LGBTQ IDENTITY people in society and disrupts the free flow of commerce.(4) Women also have faced discrimination in many establishments such as stores and restaurants, and places or establishments that provide other goods or services, such as entertainment or transportation, including sexual harassment, differential pricing for substantially similar products and services, and denial of services because they are pregnant or breastfeeding.(5) Many employers already and continue to take proactive steps, beyond those required by some States GPE and localities, to ensure they are fostering positive and respectful cultures for all employees. Many places of public accommodation also recognize the economic imperative to offer goods and services to as many consumers as possible.(6) Regular and ongoing discrimination against LGBTQ IDENTITY people, as well as women, in accessing public accommodations contributes to negative social and economic outcomes, and in the case of public accommodations operated by State ORG and local governments, abridges individuals' constitutional rights.(7) The discredited practice known as ``conversion therapy'' is a form of discrimination that harms LGBTQ IDENTITY people by undermining individuals' sense of self worth, increasing suicide ideation and substance abuse, exacerbating family conflict, and contributing to second ORDINAL -class status.(8) Both LGBTQ IDENTITY people and women face widespread discrimination in employment and various services, including by entities that receive Federal financial assistance. Such discrimination--(A ORG ) is particularly troubling and inappropriate for programs and services funded wholly or in part by the Federal Government;(B ORG ) undermines national progress toward equal treatment regardless of sex SEX , sexual orientation SEXUALITY , or gender GENDER identity; and(C PERSON ) is inconsistent with the constitutional principle of equal protection under the Fourteenth Amendment LAW to the Constitution of the United States.(9) Federal courts have widely recognized that, in enacting the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE , Congress ORG validly invoked its powers under the Fourteenth Amendment LAW to provide a full range of remedies in response to persistent, widespread, and pervasive discrimination by both private and government actors.(10) Discrimination by State ORG and local governments on the basis of sexual orientation SEXUALITY or gender GENDER identity in employment, housing, and public accommodations, and in programs and activities receiving Federal financial assistance, violates the Equal Protection Clause of ORG the Fourteenth Amendment LAW to the Constitution of the United States GPE . In many circumstances, such discrimination also violates other constitutional rights such as those of liberty and privacy under the due process clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.(11) Individuals ORG who are LGBTQ IDENTITY , or are perceived to be LGBTQ IDENTITY , have been subjected to a history and pattern of persistent, widespread, and pervasive discrimination on the bases of sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity by both private sector and Federal, State ORG , and local government actors, including in employment, housing, and public accommodations, and in programs and activities receiving Federal financial assistance. This discrimination inflicts a range of tangible and intangible harms, sometimes even including serious physical injury or death. An explicit and comprehensive national solution is needed to address this discrimination, including the full range of remedies available under the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964.(12) Discrimination based on sexual orientation SEXUALITY includes discrimination based on an individual's actual or perceived romantic, emotional, physical, or sexual attraction to other persons, or lack thereof, on the basis of gender GENDER . LGBTQ IDENTITY people, including gender GENDER nonbinary GENDER people, also commonly experience discrimination because of sex SEX -based stereotypes. Many people are subjected to discrimination because of others' perceptions or beliefs regarding their sexual orientation SEXUALITY . Even if these perceptions are incorrect, the identity imputed by others forms the basis of discrimination.(13) Numerous provisions of Federal law expressly prohibit discrimination on the basis of sex SEX , and Federal courts and agencies have correctly interpreted these prohibitions on sex SEX discrimination to include discrimination based on sexual orientation SEXUALITY , gender GENDER identity, and sex SEX stereotypes. In particular, the Supreme Court ORG of the United States GPE correctly held in Bostock GPE v. Clayton County, 140 CARDINAL S. Ct. 1731 DATE ( 2020 DATE ) that the prohibition on employment discrimination because of sex SEX under title VII of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE inherently includes discrimination because of sexual orientation SEXUALITY or transgender status.(14) This Act makes explicit that existing Federal statutes prohibiting sex SEX discrimination in employment (including in access to benefits), healthcare, housing, education, credit, and jury service also prohibit sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity discrimination.(15 PERSON ) LGBTQ IDENTITY people often face discrimination when seeking to rent or purchase housing, as well as in every other aspect of obtaining and maintaining housing. LGBTQ IDENTITY people in same- sex SEX relationships are often discriminated against when two CARDINAL names associated with one CARDINAL gender GENDER appear on a housing application, and transgender people often encounter discrimination when credit checks or inquiries reveal a former name.(16) National surveys, including a study commissioned by the Department of Housing and Urban Development ORG , show that housing discrimination against LGBTQ IDENTITY people is very prevalent. For instance, when same- sex SEX couples inquire about housing that is available for rent, they are less likely to receive positive responses from landlords. A national matched-pair testing investigation found that nearly one-half CARDINAL of same- sex SEX couples had encountered adverse, differential treatment when seeking elder housing. According to other studies, transgender people have half CARDINAL the homeownership rate of non-transgender people and about 1 CARDINAL in 5 CARDINAL transgender people experience homelessness. Another survey found that 82 percent PERCENT of gender GENDER nonbinary GENDER people experiencing homelessness lacked access to shelter.(17) As a result of the absence of explicit prohibitions against discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity, credit applicants who are LGBTQ IDENTITY , or are perceived to be LGBTQ IDENTITY , have unequal opportunities to establish credit. LGBTQ IDENTITY people can experience being denied a mortgage, credit card, student loan, or many other types of credit simply because of their sexual orientation SEXUALITY or gender GENDER identity.(18) Numerous studies demonstrate that LGBTQ IDENTITY people, especially transgender people and women, are economically disadvantaged and at a higher risk for poverty compared with other groups of people. For example, the poverty rate for older women in same- sex SEX couples is twice that of older different- sex SEX couples.(19) The right to an impartial jury of one's peers and the reciprocal right to jury service are fundamental to the free and democratic system of justice in the United States GPE and are based in the Bill of Rights LAW . There is, however, an unfortunate and long-documented history in the United States GPE of attorneys discriminating against LGBTQ IDENTITY individuals, or those perceived to be LGBTQ IDENTITY , in jury selection. Failure to bar peremptory challenges based on the actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY or gender GENDER identity of an individual not only erodes a fundamental right, duty, and obligation of being a citizen of the United States GPE , but also unfairly creates a second ORDINAL class of citizenship for LGBTQ IDENTITY victims, witnesses, plaintiffs, and defendants.(20) Numerous studies document the shortage of qualified and available homes for the approximately 424,000 CARDINAL youth in the child welfare system and the negative outcomes for the many youth who live in group care as opposed to a loving home or who age out of care without a permanent family placement. Although same- sex SEX couples are 7 CARDINAL times more likely to foster or adopt than their different- sex SEX counterparts, many child-placing agencies refuse to serve same- sex SEX couples and LGBTQ IDENTITY individuals. This has resulted in a reduction of the pool of qualified and available homes for youth in the child welfare system who need placement on a temporary or permanent basis. It also sends a negative message about LGBTQ IDENTITY people to children and youth in the child welfare system about who is, and who is not, considered fit to be a parent. While the priority should be on providing the supports necessary to keep children with their families, when removal is required, barring discrimination in foster care and adoption will increase the number of homes available to foster children waiting for foster and adoptive families.(21 GPE ) LGBTQ IDENTITY youth are overrepresented in the foster care system by at least a factor of two CARDINAL and report twice the rate of poor treatment while in care compared to their non- LGBTQ IDENTITY counterparts. LGBTQ IDENTITY youth in foster care have a higher average number of placements, higher likelihood of living in a group home, and higher rates of hospitalization for emotional reasons and of juvenile justice involvement than their non- LGBTQ IDENTITY peers because of the high level of bias and discrimination that they face and the difficulty of finding affirming foster placements. Further, due to their physical distance from friends and family, traumatic experiences, and potentially unstable living situations, all youth involved with child welfare services are at risk for being targeted by traffickers seeking to exploit children. Barring discrimination in child welfare services will ensure improved treatment and outcomes for LGBTQ IDENTITY foster children.(22) Courts consistently have found that the government has a compelling interest in preventing and remedying discrimination. For example, the Supreme Court ORG of the United States GPE found there to be a compelling government interest in eliminating sex SEX discrimination in Board of Directors of ORG Rotary International v. Rotary Club of Duarte ORG , 481 CARDINAL U.S. GPE 537, 549 CARDINAL ( 1987 DATE ). Because discrimination based on sexual orientation SEXUALITY or gender GENDER identity inherently is a form of sex SEX discrimination, as held in Bostock GPE v. Clayton County, 140 CARDINAL S. Ct. 1731 DATE ( 2020 DATE ), this Act furthers the compelling government interest in providing redress for the serious harms to mental and physical health, financial security and well-being, civic participation, freedom of movement and opportunity, personal dignity, and physical safety that result from discrimination. Consistent with the role nondiscrimination laws play in protecting lives and livelihoods, alleviating suffering, and improving individual and public health, the Supreme Court ORG of the United States GPE has long recognized, under the decision in Heart of Atlanta Motel ORG , Inc. v. United States GPE , 379 CARDINAL U.S. GPE 241 CARDINAL ( 1964 DATE ), that these laws also benefit society as a whole by ending the ``disruptive effect'' discrimination has on travel and commerce, and by creating a level field for all participants in a given sector.(23) As with all prohibitions on invidious discrimination, this Act furthers the government's compelling interest in the least restrictive way because only by forbidding discrimination is it possible to avert or redress the harms described in this subsection.(b) Purpose.--It is the purpose of this Act to expand as well as clarify, confirm and create greater consistency in the protections and remedies against discrimination on the basis of all covered characteristics and to provide guidance and notice to individuals, organizations, corporations, and agencies regarding their obligations under the law. SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . PUBLIC ACCOMMODATIONS.(a) Prohibition on Discrimination or Segregation in Public WORK_OF_ART Accommodations.--Section 201 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 U.S.C. 2000a DATE ) is amended--(1) in subsection (a), by inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''; and(2) in subsection ( b)--(A ORG ) in paragraph ( 3 CARDINAL ), by striking ``stadium'' and all that follows and inserting ``stadium or other place of or establishment that provides exhibition, entertainment, recreation, exercise, amusement, public gathering, or public display;'';(B) by redesignating paragraph ( 4 CARDINAL ) as paragraph ( 6 CARDINAL ); and(C PERSON ) by inserting after paragraph ( 3 CARDINAL ) the following:``(4) any establishment that provides a good, service, or program, including a store, shopping center, online retailer or service provider, salon, bank, gas station, food bank, service or care center, shelter, travel agency, or funeral parlor, or establishment that provides health care, accounting, or legal services;``(5) any train service, bus service, car service, taxi service, airline service, station, depot, or other place of or establishment that provides transportation service; and''.(b) Prohibition on Discrimination or Segregation Under Law.--Section 202 CARDINAL of such Act ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000a-1 CARDINAL ) is amended by inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''.(c) Rule of Construction.--Title II of such Act ( 42 U.S.C. 2000a DATE et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:``SEC GPE . 208 CARDINAL . RULE OF CONSTRUCTION.``A reference in this title to an establishment--``(1 ORG ) shall be construed to include an individual whose operations affect commerce and who is a provider of a good, service, or program; and``(2) shall not be construed to be limited to a physical facility or place.''. SEC ORG . 4. CARDINAL DESEGREGATION OF PUBLIC FACILITIES.Section 301(a) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000b(a CARDINAL )) is amended by inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''. SEC ORG . 5. CARDINAL DESEGREGATION OF PUBLIC EDUCATION.(a) Definitions.--Section 401(b) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000c(b CARDINAL )) is amended by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''.(b) Civil Actions by the Attorney General.--Section 407 CARDINAL of such Act ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000c-6 CARDINAL ) is amended, in subsection (a)(2), by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''.(c) Classification and Assignment.--Section 410 of such Act QUANTITY ( 42 DATE U.S.C. 2000c-9) is amended by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''. SEC ORG . 6 CARDINAL . FEDERAL FUNDING.Section 601 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 U.S.C. DATE 2000d DATE ) is amended by inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin,''. SEC ORG . 7 CARDINAL . EMPLOYMENT.(a) Rules of Construction.--Title VII of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE is amended by inserting after section 701 LAW ( 42 U.S.C. 2000e DATE ) the following:``SEC GPE . 701A. RULES OF CONSTRUCTION.``Section 1106 DATE shall apply to this title except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section to an `unlawful practice' shall be considered to be a reference to an `unlawful employment practice'.''.(b) Unlawful Employment ORG Practices.--Section 703 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000e-2) is amended--(1) in the section header, by striking `` sex SEX ,'' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'';(2) except in subsection (e), by striking `` sex SEX ,'' each place it appears and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'';(3) in subsection (e)(1), by striking ``enterprise,'' and inserting ``enterprise, if, in a situation in which sex SEX is a bona fide occupational qualification, individuals are recognized as qualified in accordance with their gender GENDER identity,''; and(4 PERSON ) in subsection (h), by striking `` sex SEX '' the second ORDINAL place it appears and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),''.(c) Other Unlawful Employment Practices.--Section 704(b) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000e-3(b CARDINAL )) is amended--(1) by striking `` sex SEX ,'' the first ORDINAL place it appears and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),''; and(2) by striking ``employment.'' and inserting ``employment, if, in a situation in which sex SEX is a bona fide occupational qualification, individuals are recognized as qualified in accordance with their gender GENDER identity.''.(d) Claims.--Section 706(g)(2)(A CARDINAL ) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 2000e-5(g)(2)(A CARDINAL )) is amended by striking `` sex SEX ,'' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),''.(e) Employment by Federal Government.--Section 717 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000e-16 DATE ) is amended--(1) in subsection (a), by striking `` sex SEX ,'' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),''; and(2) in subsection (c), by striking `` sex SEX '' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),''.(f) Government Employee Rights Act of 1991.--The Government Employee Rights Act LAW of 1991 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000e-16a CARDINAL et seq.) is amended--(1) in section 301(b), by striking `` sex SEX ,'' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'';(2) in section 302(a)(1 LAW ), by striking `` sex SEX ,'' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),''; and(3) by adding at the end the following:``SEC GPE . 305 CARDINAL . RULES OF CONSTRUCTION AND CLAIMS.``Sections 1101(b), 1106 DATE , and 1107 DATE of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this title except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section 1106 DATE to `race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin' shall be considered to be a reference to `race, color, religion, sex SEX , sexual orientation SEXUALITY , gender GENDER identity, national origin, age, or disability'.''.(g) Congressional Accountability Act LAW of 1995.--The Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 ( 2 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1301 DATE et seq.) is amended--(1) in section 201(a)(1 LAW ) ( 2 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1311(a)(1 CARDINAL )) by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin,''; and(2) by adding at the end of title II ( 42 U.S.C. DATE 1311 DATE et seq.) the following:``SEC GPE . 209 CARDINAL . RULES OF CONSTRUCTION AND CLAIMS.``Sections 1101(b), 1106 DATE , and 1107 DATE of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to section 201 LAW (and remedial provisions of this Act related to section 201 LAW ) except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section 1106 DATE to `race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin' shall be considered to be a reference to `race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), national origin, age, or disability'.''.(h) Civil Service Reform Act of 1978.--Chapter 23 CARDINAL of title 5, United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended--(1) in section 2301(b)(2 LAW ), by striking `` sex SEX ,'' and inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'';(2) in section 2302--(A) in subsection (b)(1)(A), by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin,''; and(B ORG ) in subsection (d)(1), by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin;''; and(3) by adding at the end the following:``SEC GPE . 2307 DATE . RULES OF CONSTRUCTION AND CLAIMS.``Sections 1101(b), 1106 DATE , and 1107 DATE of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this chapter (and remedial provisions of this title related to this chapter) except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section 1106 DATE to `race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin' shall be considered to be a reference to `race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), national origin, age, a handicapping condition, marital status, or political affiliation'.''. SEC ORG . 8 CARDINAL . INTERVENTION.Section 902 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000h-2 CARDINAL ) is amended by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin,''. SEC ORG . 9 CARDINAL . MISCELLANEOUS.Title XI of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE is amended--(1) by redesignating sections 1101 through 1104 DATE ( 42 U.S.C. DATE 2000h DATE et seq.) and sections 1105 DATE and 1106 DATE ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000h-5 CARDINAL , 2000h-6) as sections 1102 through 1105 DATE and sections 1108 and 1109 DATE , respectively;(2) by inserting after the title heading the following:``SEC GPE . 1101 DATE . DEFINITIONS ORG AND RULES.``(a ORG ) Definitions.--In titles II CARDINAL , III GPE , IV GPE , VI ORG , VII, and IX (referred to individually in sections 1106 DATE and 1107 DATE as a `covered title'):``(1) Race; color; religion; sex SEX ; sexual orientation SEXUALITY ; gender GENDER identity; national origin.--The term `race', `color', `religion', ` sex SEX ' (including `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' and ` gender GENDER identity'), or `national origin', used with respect to an individual, includes--``(A) the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin, respectively, of another person with whom the individual is associated or has been associated; and``(B) a perception or belief, even if inaccurate, concerning the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin, respectively, of the individual.``(2) Gender identity.--The term ` gender GENDER identity' means the gender GENDER -related identity, appearance, mannerisms, or other gender GENDER -related characteristics of an individual, regardless of the individual's designated sex SEX at birth.``(3) Including.--The term `including' means including, but not limited to, consistent with the term's standard meaning in Federal law. ORG ``(4) Sex.--The term ` sex SEX ' includes--``(A) a sex SEX stereotype;``(B) pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition;``(C) sexual orientation SEXUALITY or gender GENDER identity; and``(D) sex SEX characteristics, including intersex traits.``(5) Sexual orientation.--The term `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' means homosexuality, heterosexuality, or bisexuality.``(b) Rules.--In a covered title referred to in subsection (a)--``(1) (with respect to sex SEX ) pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition shall not receive less favorable treatment than other physical conditions; and``(2) (with respect to gender GENDER identity) an individual shall not be denied access to a shared facility, including a restroom, a locker room, and a dressing room, that is in accordance with the individual's gender GENDER identity.''; and(3) by inserting after section 1105 LAW the following:``SEC GPE . 1106 DATE . RULES OF CONSTRUCTION.``(a) Sex.--Nothing in section 1101 DATE or the provisions of a covered title incorporating a term defined or a rule specified in that section shall be construed--``(1) to limit the protection against an unlawful practice on the basis of pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition provided by section 701(k); or``(2) to limit the protection against an unlawful practice on the basis of sex SEX available under any provision of Federal law other than that covered title, prohibiting a practice on the basis of sex SEX .``(b) Claims and Remedies Not Precluded.--Nothing in section 1101 DATE or a covered title shall be construed to limit the claims or remedies available to any individual for an unlawful practice on the basis of race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin including claims brought pursuant to section 1979 DATE or 1980 DATE of the Revised Statutes FAC ( 42 U.S.C. 1983, 1985 DATE ) or any other law, including a Federal ORG law amended by the Equality Act LAW , regulation, or policy.``(c) No Negative Inference.--Nothing in section 1101 DATE or a covered title shall be construed to support any inference that any Federal law prohibiting a practice on the basis of sex SEX does not prohibit discrimination on the basis of pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition, sexual orientation SEXUALITY , gender GENDER identity, or a sex SEX stereotype.``SEC. 1107 DATE . CLAIMS.``The Religious Freedom Restoration Act LAW of 1993 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000bb et seq.) shall not provide a claim concerning, or a defense to a claim under, a covered title, or provide a basis for challenging the application or enforcement of a covered title.''. SEC ORG . 10 CARDINAL . HOUSING.(a ORG ) Fair Housing Act.--The Fair Housing Act ORG ( 42 U.S.C. DATE 3601 CARDINAL et seq.) is amended--(1) in section 802 LAW ( 42 U.S.C. 3602 DATE ), by adding at the end the following:``(p) `Gender identity', ` sex SEX ', and `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' have the meanings given those terms in section 1101(a) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964.``(q CARDINAL ) `Race', `color', `religion', ` sex SEX ' (including `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' and ` gender GENDER identity'), `handicap', `familial status', or `national origin', used with respect to an individual, includes--``(1 ORG ) the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), handicap, familial status, or national origin, respectively, of another person with whom the individual is associated or has been associated; and``(2) a perception or belief, even if inaccurate, concerning the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), handicap, familial status, or national origin, respectively, of the individual.'';(2) in section 804 LAW , by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after `` sex SEX ,'' each place that term appears;(3) in section 805 LAW , by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after `` sex SEX ,'' each place that term appears;(4) in section 806 LAW , by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after ``sex,'';(5) in section 808(e)(6), by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after `` sex SEX ,''; and(6 ORG ) by adding at the end the following:``SEC GPE . 821 CARDINAL . RULES OF CONSTRUCTION.``Sections 1101(b) and 1106 DATE of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this title and section 901 LAW , except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section 1101(b LAW ) or 1106 DATE to a `covered title' shall be considered a reference to `this title and section 901'.``SEC. 822 CARDINAL . CLAIMS.``Section 1107 of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this title and section 901 LAW , except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section 1107 to a `covered title' shall be considered a reference to `this title and section 901'.''.(b) Prevention of Intimidation ORG in Fair Housing Cases.--Section 901 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1968 ( 42 U.S.C. 3631 DATE ) is amended by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY (as such term is defined in section 802 LAW of this Act) and gender GENDER identity (as such term is defined in section 802 LAW of this Act)),'' after `` sex SEX ,'' each place that term appears. SEC ORG . 11 CARDINAL . EQUAL ORG CREDIT OPPORTUNITY.(a) Prohibited Discrimination.--Section 701(a)(1) of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ORG ( 15 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1691(a)(1 CARDINAL )) is amended by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after ``sex''.(b) Definitions.--Section 702 CARDINAL of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ORG ( 15 U.S.C. 1691a DATE ) is amended--(1) by redesignating subsections (f) and (g) as subsections (h) and (i), respectively;(2) by inserting after subsection (e) the following:``(f) The terms ` gender GENDER identity', ` sex SEX ', and `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' have the meanings given those terms in section 1101(a) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964.``(g) The term `race', `color', `religion', `national origin', ` sex SEX ' (including `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' and ` gender GENDER identity'), `marital status', or `age', used with respect to an individual, includes--``(1 ORG ) the race, color, religion, national origin, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), marital status, or age, respectively, of another person with whom the individual is associated or has been associated; and``(2) a perception or belief, even if inaccurate, concerning the race, color, religion, national origin, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), marital status, or age, respectively, of the individual.''; and(3 PERSON ) by adding at the end the following:``(j) Sections 1101(b) and 1106 DATE of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this title, except that for purposes of that application--``(1) a reference in those sections to a `covered title' shall be considered a reference to `this title'; and``(2) paragraph ( 1 CARDINAL ) of such section 1101(b) shall apply with respect to all aspects of a credit transaction.''.(c) Relation to State Laws.--Section 705(a) of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ORG ( 15 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1691d(a)) is amended by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after ``sex''.(d) Civil Liability.--Section 706 CARDINAL of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ORG ( 15 U.S.C. 1691e DATE ) is amended by adding at the end the following:``(l) Section 1107 ORG of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this title, except that for purposes of that application, a reference in that section to a `covered title' shall be considered a reference to `this title'.''. SEC ORG . 12 CARDINAL . JURIES.(a ORG ) In General.--Chapter 121 CARDINAL of title 28 CARDINAL , United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended--(1) in section 1862 LAW , by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after ``sex,'';(2) in section 1867(e LAW ), in the second ORDINAL sentence, by inserting ``(including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' after ``sex,'';(3) in section 1869 DATE --(A) in subsection (j), by striking ``and'' at the end;(B) in subsection (k), by striking the period at the end and inserting a semicolon; and(C PERSON ) by adding at the end the following:``(l) ` gender GENDER identity', ` sex SEX ', and `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' have the meanings given such terms under section 1101(a LAW ) of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE ; and``(m) `race', `color', `religion', ` sex SEX ' (including `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' and ` gender GENDER identity'), `economic status', or `national origin', used with respect to an individual, includes--``(1 ORG ) the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), economic status, or national origin, respectively, of another person with whom the individual is associated or has been associated; and``(2) a perception or belief, even if inaccurate, concerning the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), economic status, or national origin, respectively, of the individual.''; and(4 PERSON ) by adding at the end the following:``Sec. 1879 DATE . Rules of construction and claims``Sections 1101(b), 1106 DATE , and 1107 DATE of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 DATE shall apply to this chapter, except that for purposes of that application, a reference in those sections to a `covered title' shall be considered a reference to `this chapter'.''.(b) Technical and Conforming Amendment.--The table of sections for chapter 121 LAW of title 28 CARDINAL , United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended by adding at the end the following:``1879. Rules of construction and claims.''. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. ORG 1399 DATE Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 1399 To amend chapter 110 LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code, to prohibit gender GENDER -affirming care on minors, and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES March 7, 2023Ms ORG . Greene PERSON of Georgia GPE (for herself, Mrs. Miller PERSON of Illinois GPE , Mr. Gosar PERSON , Mr. Gooden PERSON of Texas GPE , Mr. Grothman PERSON , Mr. Duncan PERSON , Mrs. Luna PERSON , Mrs. Harshbarger PERSON , Mr. Norman PERSON , Mr. Nehls PERSON , Mr. Good PERSON of Virginia GPE , Mr. Hern PERSON , Mr. Rosendale PERSON , Mr. Babin PERSON , Mr. Lamborn PERSON , Mr. Higgins PERSON of Louisiana GPE , Mr. Steube PERSON , Ms. Hageman PERSON , Mrs. Lesko PERSON , and Mr. Biggs PERSON ) introduced thefollowing bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary ORG , and in addition to the Committees on Ways and Means, Energy andCommerce ORG , and Education ORG and the Workforce ORG , for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committeeconcerned A BILLTo amend chapter 110 LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code, to prohibit gender GENDER -affirming care on minors, and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Protect Children's Innocence Act''. PRODUCT TITLE I--GENDER-AFFIRMING CARE ON MINORS PROHIBITEDSEC. 101 CARDINAL . GENDER-AFFIRMING CARE ON MINORS PROHIBITED.Chapter ORG 110 CARDINAL of title 18, United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended--(1) by adding at the end the following:``Sec. 2260B. CARDINAL Gender-affirming care on minors``(a) In General.--Whoever, in any circumstance described in subsection (e), knowingly performs any gender GENDER -affirming care on a minor is guilty of a class C felony.``(b) Prohibition on Prosecution of Person on Whom Intervention Is Performed.--No person on whom the gender GENDER -affirming care under subsection (a) is performed may be arrested or prosecuted for an offense under this section.``(c) Civil Action.--A person on whom gender GENDER -affirming care is performed under this section may bring a civil action for appropriate relief, including compensatory and punitive damages, against each person who performed the gender GENDER -affirming care.``(d) Definitions.--In this section:``(1) Gender-affirming care.--``(A) In general.--For purposes of this chapter, except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term ` gender GENDER -affirming care' means, with respect to an individual, any of the following:``(i) Performing any surgery for the purpose of changing the body of such individual to correspond to a sex SEX that differs from their biological SEX sex SEX , including--``(I) castration;``(II) orchiectomy;``(III) scrotoplasty;``(IV) vasectomy;``(V) hysterectomy;``(VI) oophorectomy;``(VII) ovariectomy;``(VIII) metoidioplasty;``(IX) penectomy;``(X) phalloplasty;``(XI) vaginoplasty;``(XII) vaginectomy;``(XIII) vulvoplasty;``(XIV ORG ) reduction thyrochondroplasty;``(XV) chondrolaryngoplasty; and``(XVI) mastectomy.``(ii) Any plastic surgery that feminizes or masculinizes the facial features for the purposes described in clause (i).``(iii) Any placement of chest implants to create feminine breasts for the purposes described in clause (i).``(iv) Any placement of fat or artificial implants in the gluteal region for the purposes described in clause (i).``(v) Administering, supplying, prescribing, dispensing, distributing, or otherwise conveying to an individual medications for the purposes described in clause (i), including--``(I) gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) analogues or other puberty-blocking drugs to stop or delay normal puberty;``(II) testosterone or other androgens to biological SEX females at doses that are supraphysiologic to the female GENDER sex SEX ; and``(III GPE ) estrogen ORDINAL to biological SEX males at doses that are supraphysiologic to the male GENDER sex SEX .``(B) Exception.--Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the following individuals:``(i GPE ) An individual with both ovarian and testicular tissue.``(ii) An individual with respect to whom a physician has determined through genetic or biochemical testing that the individual does not have normal sex SEX chromosome structure, sex SEX steroid hormone production, or sex SEX steroid hormone action.``(iii) An individual experiencing infection, disease, injury, or disorder caused or exacerbated by previous gender GENDER transition procedures.``(iv) An individual suffering from a physical disorder, physical injury, or physical illness that would, as certified by a physician, place the individual in imminent danger of death or impairment of a major bodily function unless the procedure is performed.``(2) Biological sex.--The term ` biological SEX sex SEX ' means the indication of male GENDER or female GENDER sex SEX by reproductive potential or capacity, sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, gonads, or internal or external genitalia present at birth.``(3) Minor.--The term `minor' means any person under the age of eighteen years DATE .``(e) Circumstances Described.--For purposes of subsection (a), the circumstances described in this subsection are that--``(1 PERSON ) the defendant or victim traveled in interstate or foreign commerce, or traveled using a means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce, in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a);``(2) the defendant used a means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a);``(3) any payment of any kind was made, directly or indirectly, in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a) using any means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce or in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce;``(4) the defendant transmitted in interstate or foreign commerce any communication relating to or in furtherance of the conduct described in subsection (a) using any means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce or in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce by any means or in manner, including by computer, mail, wire, or electromagnetic transmission;``(5) any instrument, item, substance, or other object that has traveled in interstate or foreign commerce was used to perform the conduct described in subsection (a);``(6) the conduct described in subsection (a) occurred within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States GPE , or any territory or possession of the United States GPE ; or``(7 GPE ) the conduct described in subsection (a) otherwise occurred in or affected interstate or foreign commerce.``(f) Rule of Construction.--Nothing in this section shall be construed as prohibiting provision of the medical services described in subsection (d)(1)(A) to address legitimate health issues, such as any male GENDER or female GENDER reproductive cancers, apart from changing the body to correspond to a sex SEX that differs from one's biological SEX sex SEX .''; and(2) by amending the table of sections for such chapter by adding at the end the following:``2260B. Gender-affirming care on minors.''.TITLE II--PROHIBITING FEDERALLY FUNDED GENDER-AFFIRMING CARESEC. 201 CARDINAL . PROHIBITING TAXPAYER-FUNDED GENDER-AFFIRMING CARE.Title 1, United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended by adding at the end the following new chapter: `` CHAPTER 4--PROHIBITING LAW TAXPAYER-FUNDED GENDER-AFFIRMING CARE``301. Prohibition on funding for gender GENDER -affirming care.``302. Prohibition on funding for health benefits plans that cover gender GENDER -affirming care.``303. Limitation on Federal facilities and employees, Federal lands and territories, and Tribal ORG territories.``304. Construction relating to separate coverage.``305. Construction relating to the use of non-Federal funds for health coverage.``306. Construction relating to complications arising from gender GENDER -affirming care. ``307 PERSON . Treatment of individuals born with medically verifiable disorder of sex SEX development.``308. Gender-affirming care defined. ``309 DATE . Rule of construction.``Sec. 301 CARDINAL . Prohibition on funding for gender GENDER -affirming care``No funds authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are authorized or appropriated by Federal law, including funds provided under titles XVIII, XIX ORG , and XXI ORG of the Social Security Act, shall be expended for any gender GENDER -affirming care.``Sec. 302 CARDINAL . Prohibition on funding for health benefits plans that cover gender GENDER -affirming care``No funds authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are authorized or appropriated by Federal law, shall be expended for health benefits coverage that includes coverage of gender GENDER -affirming care.``Sec. 303 CARDINAL . Limitation on Federal facilities and employees, Federal lands and territories, and Tribal ORG territories``No health care service furnished--``(1 PERSON ) by or in a health care facility owned or operated by the Federal Government ORG , Federal land or territory, or a Tribal ORG territory; or``(2) by any physician or other individual employed by the Federal Government ORG , Federal land or territory, or a Tribal ORG territory to provide health care services within the scope of the physician's or individual's employment,may include gender GENDER -affirming care.``Sec. 304 CARDINAL . Construction relating to separate coverage``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as prohibiting any individual, entity, or State ORG or locality from purchasing separate coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care or health benefits coverage that includes gender GENDER -affirming care so long as such coverage is paid for entirely using only funds not authorized or appropriated by Federal law, federal programs ORG , platforms, or infrastructure, such coverage does not cover any practice that would be subject to penalty under section 2260B LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code PERSON , and such coverage shall not be purchased using matching funds required for a federally subsidized program, including a State ORG 's or locality's contribution of Medicaid ORG matching funds.``Sec. 305 CARDINAL . Construction relating to the use of non-Federal funds for health coverage``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as restricting the ability of any non-Federal ORG health benefits coverage provider from offering coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care, or the ability of a State ORG or locality to contract separately with such a provider for such coverage, so long as only funds not authorized or appropriated by Federal law are used, such coverage does not cover any practice that would be subject to penalty under section 2260B LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code PERSON , and such coverage shall not be purchased using matching funds required for a federally subsidized program, including a State ORG 's or locality's contribution of Medicaid ORG matching funds.``Sec. 306 CARDINAL . Construction relating to complications arising from gender GENDER -affirming care``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to apply to the treatment of any infection, injury, disease, or disorder that has been caused by or exacerbated by the performance of a gender GENDER -affirming care. This rule of construction shall be applicable without regard to whether the gender GENDER -affirming care was performed in accord with Federal or State ORG law, and without regard to whether funding for the gender GENDER -affirming care is permissible under section 307.``Sec LAW . 307 CARDINAL . Treatment of individuals born with medically verifiable disorder of sex SEX development``The limitations established in sections 301 CARDINAL , 302 CARDINAL , and 303 CARDINAL shall not apply with respect to an individual described in section 2260B(d)(1)(B LAW ) of title 18 DATE , United States ORG Code.``Sec. 308. Gender-affirming care defined``For purposes of this chapter, the term ` gender GENDER -affirming care' has the meaning given such term in section 2260B(d)(1 LAW ) of title 18, United States ORG Code.``Sec. 309 CARDINAL . Rule of construction``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as prohibiting provision of the medical services described in section 2260B(d)(1)(A LAW ) of title 18, United States ORG Code, to address any male GENDER or female GENDER reproductive cancers, apart from changing the body to correspond to a sex SEX that differs from one's biological SEX sex SEX .''. SEC ORG . 202 CARDINAL . AMENDMENT PERSON TO TABLE OF CHAPTERS.The table of chapters for title 1 CARDINAL , United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended by adding at the end the following new item:``4. Prohibiting taxpayer-funded gender GENDER -affirming care...... 301'' CARDINAL .TITLE III--APPLICATION UNDER THE AFFORDABLE CARE ACTSEC. 301 CARDINAL . CLARIFYING APPLICATION OF PROHIBITION TO PREMIUM CREDITS AND COST-SHARING REDUCTIONS UNDER ACA.(a PRODUCT ) In General.--(1) Disallowance of refundable credit and cost-sharing reductions for coverage under qualified health plan which provides coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care.--(A) In general.--Subparagraph (A) of section 36B(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE is amended by inserting before the period at the end the following: ``or any health plan that includes coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care (other than any gender GENDER -affirming care or treatment described in section 306 or LAW 307 CARDINAL of title 1 CARDINAL , United States GPE Code)''.(B) Option to purchase or offer separate coverage or plan.--Paragraph ( 3 CARDINAL ) of section 36B(c LAW ) of such Code PERSON is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:``(C) Separate coverage or plan for gender GENDER -affirming care allowed.--``(i) Option to purchase separate coverage or plan.--Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall be construed as prohibiting any individual from purchasing separate coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care described in such subparagraph, or a health plan that includes such gender GENDER -affirming care, so long as no credit is allowed under this section with respect to the premiums for such coverage or plan and such coverage or plan does not cover any practice that would be subject to penalty under section 2260B LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code.``(ii) Option to offer coverage or plan.--Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall restrict any non-Federal ORG health insurance issuer offering a health plan from offering separate coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care described in such subparagraph, or a plan that includes such gender GENDER -affirming care, so long as premiums for such separate coverage or plan are not paid for with any amount attributable to the credit allowed under this section (or the amount of any advance payment of the credit under section 1412 LAW of the Patient Protection ORG and Affordable Care Act) and such coverage or plan does not cover any practice that would be subject to penalty under section 2260B LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code.''.(2) Disallowance of small employer health insurance expense credit for plan which includes coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care.--Subsection (h) of section 45R CARDINAL of the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE is amended--(A ORG ) by striking ``Any term'' and inserting the following:``(1) In general.--Any term''; and(B ORG ) by adding at the end the following new paragraph:``(2) Exclusion of health plans including coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care.--``(A) In general.--The term `qualified health plan' does not include any health plan that includes coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care (other than any gender GENDER -affirming care or treatment described in section 306 or LAW 307 CARDINAL of title 1 CARDINAL , United States GPE Code).``(B) Separate coverage or plan for gender GENDER -affirming care allowed.--``(i) Option to purchase separate coverage or plan.--Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall be construed as prohibiting any employer from purchasing for its employees separate coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care described in such subparagraph, or a health plan that includes such gender GENDER -affirming care, so long as no credit is allowed under this section with respect to the employer contributions for such coverage or plan and such coverage does not cover any practice that would be subject to penalty under section 2260B LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code.``(ii) Option to offer coverage or plan.--Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall restrict any non-Federal ORG health insurance issuer offering a health plan from offering separate coverage for gender GENDER -affirming care described in such subparagraph, or a plan that includes such gender GENDER -affirming care, so long as such separate coverage or plan is not paid for with any employer contribution eligible for the credit allowed under this section and such coverage or plan does not cover any practice that would be subject to penalty under section 2260B LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code.''.(b) Application to Multi-State Plans.--Section 1334(a GPE ) of Public Law 111 CARDINAL -148 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 18054(a GPE )) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:``(8) Coverage consistent with federal policy regarding gender GENDER -affirming care.--In LOC entering into contracts under this subsection, the Director shall ensure that no multi-State qualified health plan offered in an Exchange ORG provides health benefits coverage for which the expenditure of Federal funds is prohibited under chapter 4 LAW of title 1, United States ORG Code.''.(c) Effective Date.--The amendments made by subsection (a) shall apply to taxable years ending after the date DATE that is one year DATE after the date of enactment of this Act, but only with respect to plan years beginning after such date, and the amendment made by subsection (b) shall apply to plan years beginning after such date. TITLE ORG IV--ADDITIONAL PROVISIONSSEC. 401 CARDINAL . PROHIBITION DATE ON INSTITUTIONS OF HIGHER EDUCATION AND ACCREDITING AGENCIES OR ASSOCIATIONS.(a) Prohibition on Institutions of Higher WORK_OF_ART Education.--Section 487(a) of the Higher Education Act LAW of 1965 DATE ( 20 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1094(a GPE )) is amended by adding at the end the following:``(30) The institution will not offer instruction in gender GENDER -affirming care (as defined in section 2260B(d LAW ) of title 18 DATE , United States GPE Code).''.(b) Prohibition on Accrediting Agencies or Associations.--Section 496(a) of the Higher Education Act LAW of 1965 DATE ( 20 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1099b(a)) is amended--(1) by striking ``and'' at the end of paragraph (7);(2) by striking the period at the end of paragraph ( 8) DATE and inserting ``; and''; and(3) by adding at the end the following:``(9) such agency or association does not accredit any institution that offers instruction in gender GENDER -affirming care (as defined in section 2260B(d LAW ) of title 18 DATE , United States GPE Code).''. SEC ORG . 402 CARDINAL . IMMIGRATION CONSEQUENCES WITH RESPECT TO PROVIDING GENDER-AFFIRMING CARE.(a) Definitions.--Section 101(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act ORG (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following:``(53) The term ` gender GENDER -affirming care' shall have the meaning given such term in section 2260B(d LAW ) of title 18 DATE , United States ORG Code.''.(b) Classes of Aliens Ineligible for Visas GPE or Admission.--Section 212(a)(1)(A CARDINAL ) of the Immigration and Nationality Act ORG ( 8 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1182(a)(1)(A)) is amended--(1) in clause (iii)(II), strike ``or'' at the end;(2) in clause (iv), strike the comma at the end and insert ``, or''; and(3) by adding at the end the following:``(v) who is determined to have performed gender GENDER -affirming care on a child that has not attained the age of 18 years DATE old,''.(c) Classes of Deportable Aliens.--Section 237(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act ORG ( 8 U.S.C. 1227(a DATE )) is amended by adding at the end the following:``(8) Gender-affirming care.--Any ORG alien who has performed gender GENDER -affirming care on a child that has not attained the age of 18 years old DATE is deportable.''. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. ORG 1276 DATE Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 1276To protect children from medical malpractice in the form of gendertransition procedures.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES March 1, ORG 2023 Mr. Banks PERSON (for himself, Mr. Babin PERSON , Mr. Johnson PERSON of Louisiana GPE , Mr. Grothman PERSON , and Mr. Norman PERSON ) introduced the following bill; which wasreferred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce ORG , and in addition tothe Committees on the Judiciary, Education and the Workforce, Natural Resources ORG , and Ways and Means ORG , for a period to be subsequentlydetermined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of suchprovisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned A BILL To protect children from medical malpractice in the form of gendertransition procedures.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Protect Minors from Medical Malpractice Act PRODUCT of 2023'' CARDINAL . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION FOR A GENDER-TRANSITION PROCEDURE PERFORMED ON A MINOR.(a ORG ) In General.--A medical practitioner, in any circumstance described in subsection (c), who performs a gender GENDER -transition procedure on an individual who is less than 18 years of age shall DATE , as described in subsection (b), be liable to the individual if injured (including any physical, psychological, emotional, or physiological harms) by such procedure, related treatment, or the after effects of the procedure or treatment.(b) Private Right of Action.--An individual covered by subsection (a) who receives a gender GENDER -transition procedure from a medical practitioner (or a representative, including a legal guardian, on behalf of such individual) may, not later than the day that is 30 years DATE after the date on which the individual turns 18 years of age DATE , bring a civil action against such medical practitioner in a court of competent jurisdiction for--(1) declaratory or injunctive relief;(2) compensatory damages;(3) punitive damages; and(4 GPE ) attorney's fees and costs.(c) Circumstances.--For the purposes of subsection (a), the circumstances described in this subsection are that--(1) the medical practitioner or the individual receiving the gender GENDER -transition procedure traveled in interstate or foreign commerce, or traveled using a means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce, in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a);(2) the medical practitioner used a means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a);(3) any payment of any kind was made, directly or indirectly, in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a) using any means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce or in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce;(4) the medical practitioner transmitted in interstate or foreign commerce any communication relating to or in furtherance of the conduct described in subsection (a) using any means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce or in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce by any means or in any manner, including by computer, mail, wire, or electromagnetic transmission;(5) any instrument, item, substance, or other object that has traveled in interstate or foreign commerce was used to perform the conduct described in subsection (a);(6) the conduct described in subsection (a) occurred within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States GPE , or any territory or possession of the United States GPE ; or(7 GPE ) the conduct described in subsection (a) otherwise occurred in or affected interstate or foreign commerce. SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . PRESERVING FREEDOM OF CONSCIENCE AND MEDICAL JUDGEMENT FOR MEDICAL PROVIDERS.Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no provision of Federal law shall require, or be construed to require, a medical practitioner to perform a gender GENDER -transition procedure. SEC ORG . 4 CARDINAL . PROHIBITION DATE ON FUNDING FOR CERTAIN STATES.Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any State ORG that requires medical practitioners to perform any gender GENDER -transition procedure on an individual in the State ORG shall be ineligible to receive any Federal funding from the Department of Health and Human Services ORG . SEC ORG . 5 CARDINAL . DEFINITIONS.In this Act:(1) Biological sex.--The term `` biological SEX sex SEX '' means the genetic classification of an individual as male GENDER or female GENDER , as reflected in the organization of the body of such individual for a reproductive role or capacity, such as through sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, and internal and external genitalia present at birth, without regard to the subjective sense of identity of the individual.(2) Gender-transition procedure.--(A) In general.--Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term `` gender GENDER -transition procedure'' means--(i) the prescription or administration of puberty-blocking drugs for the purpose of changing the body of an individual so that it conforms to the subjective sense of identity of the individual, in the case such identity is at odds with the individual's biological SEX sex;(ii) the prescription or administration of cross- sex SEX hormones for the purpose of changing the body of an individual so that it conforms to the subjective sense of identity of the individual, in the case such identity is at odds with the individual's biological SEX sex SEX ; or(iii) a surgery to change the body of an individual so that it conforms to the subjective sense of identity of the individual, in the case such identity is at odds with the individual's biological SEX sex.(B) Exception.--The term `` gender GENDER -transition procedure'' does not include--(i) an intervention described in subparagraph (A) that is performed on--(I) an individual with biological SEX sex SEX characteristics that are inherently ambiguous, such as those born with 46 CARDINAL XX chromosomes with virilization, 46 CARDINAL XY ORG chromosomes with undervirilization, or having both ovarian and testicular tissue; or(II) an individual with respect to whom a physician has determined through genetic or biochemical testing that the individual does not have normal sex SEX chromosome structure, sex SEX steroid hormone production, or sex SEX steroid hormone action, for a biological SEX male GENDER or biological SEX female;(ii) the treatment of any infection, injury, disease, or disorder that has been caused or exacerbated by the performance of an intervention described in subparagraph (A) without regard to whether the intervention was performed in accordance with State ORG or Federal law or whether the intervention is covered by the private right of action under section 2 LAW ; or(iii) any procedure undertaken because the individual suffers from a physical disorder, physical injury, or physical illness that would, as certified by a physician, place the individual in imminent danger of death or impairment of major bodily function unless the procedure is performed.(3) Medical practitioner.--The term ``medical practitioner'' means a person who is licensed, certified, or otherwise authorized by the laws of a State ORG to administer health care in the ordinary course of the practice of the person's profession. SEC ORG . 6 CARDINAL . EFFECTIVE DATE.This Act shall take effect on the date of enactment of this Act. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 4393 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH.
