R. 4393To prohibit discrimination in public accommodations on the basis ofsex, gender GENDER identity, and sexual orientation SEXUALITY , and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES June 27, ORG 2023 Mrs. Watson Coleman PERSON (for herself, Mr. Carson PERSON , Mr. Pocan PERSON , Ms. Norton PERSON , Mrs. Beatty PERSON , Ms. Tokuda PERSON , Ms. Lee PERSON of California GPE , Ms. Williams PERSON ofGeorgia, Mr. Grijalva PERSON , Mr. Espaillat PERSON , Mr. Johnson PERSON of Georgia GPE , and Mr. Carter PERSON of Louisiana GPE ) introduced the following bill; which was referredto the Committee on the Judiciary A ORG BILL To prohibit discrimination in public accommodations on the basis ofsex, gender GENDER identity, and sexual orientation SEXUALITY , and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Customer Non-Discrimination Act'' PRODUCT . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . PUBLIC ACCOMMODATIONS.(a) Prohibition on Discrimination or Segregation in Public WORK_OF_ART Accommodations.--Section 201 CARDINAL of the Civil Rights Act LAW of 1964 ( 42 U.S.C. 2000a DATE ) is amended--(1) in subsection (a), by inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''; and(2) in subsection ( b)--(A ORG ) in paragraph ( 3 CARDINAL ), by striking ``stadium'' and all that follows and inserting ``stadium or other place of or establishment that provides exhibition, entertainment, recreation, exercise, amusement, gathering, or display;'';(B) by redesignating paragraph ( 4 CARDINAL ) as paragraph ( 6 CARDINAL ); and(C PERSON ) by inserting after paragraph ( 3 CARDINAL ) the following:``(4) any establishment that provides a good, service, or program, including a store, shopping center, online retailer or service provider, salon, bank, gas station, food bank, service or care center, shelter, travel agency, or funeral parlor, or establishment that provides health care, accounting, or legal services;``(5) any train service, bus service, car service, taxi service, airline service, station, depot, or other place of or establishment that provides transportation service; and''.(b) Prohibition on Discrimination or Segregation Under Law.--Section 202 CARDINAL of such Act ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000a-1 CARDINAL ) is amended by inserting `` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity),'' before ``or national origin''.(c) Definitions and Rules of Construction.--Title II of such Act QUANTITY ( 42 U.S.C. 2000a DATE et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:``SEC GPE . 208 CARDINAL . DEFINITIONS AND RULES.``(a) Definitions.--``(1 PERSON ) Race; color; religion; sex SEX ; sexual orientation SEXUALITY ; gender GENDER identity; national origin.--The term `race', `color', `religion', ` sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity)', or `national origin', used with respect to an individual, includes--``(A) the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin, respectively, of another person with whom the individual is associated or has been associated; and``(B) a perception or belief, even if inaccurate, concerning the race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin, respectively, of the individual.``(2) Gender identity.--The term ` gender GENDER identity' means the gender GENDER -related identity, appearance, mannerisms, or other gender GENDER -related characteristics of an individual, regardless of the individual's designated sex SEX at birth.``(3) Including.--The term `including' means including, but not limited to, consistent with the term's standard meaning in Federal law. ORG ``(4) Sex.--The term ` sex SEX ' includes--``(A) a sex SEX stereotype;``(B) pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition;``(C) sexual orientation SEXUALITY or gender GENDER identity; and``(D) sex SEX characteristics, including intersex traits.``(5) Sexual orientation.--The term `sexual orientation SEXUALITY ' means homosexuality, heterosexuality, or bisexuality.``(b) Rules.--In this title--``(1) (with respect to sex SEX ) pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition shall not receive less favorable treatment than other physical conditions; and``(2) (with respect to gender GENDER identity) an individual shall not be denied access to a shared facility, including a restroom, a locker room, and a dressing room, that is in accordance with the individual's gender GENDER identity.``SEC. 209 CARDINAL . RULES OF CONSTRUCTION.``(a) Claims and Remedies Not Precluded.--Nothing in this title shall be construed to limit the claims or remedies available to any individual for an unlawful practice on the basis of race, color, religion, sex SEX (including sexual orientation SEXUALITY and gender GENDER identity), or national origin including claims brought pursuant to section 1979 DATE or 1980 DATE of the Revised Statutes FAC ( 42 U.S.C. 1983, 1985 DATE ) or any other law, including the Federal ORG law amended by the Customer Non-Discrimination Act ORG , regulation, or policy.``(b) No Negative Inference.--Nothing in this title shall be construed to support any inference that any Federal law prohibiting a practice on the basis of sex SEX does not prohibit discrimination on the basis of pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition, sexual orientation SEXUALITY , gender GENDER identity, or a sex SEX stereotype.``(c) Scope of an Establishment.--A reference in this title to an establishment--``(1 ORG ) shall be construed to include an individual whose operations affect commerce and who is a provider of a good, service, or program; and``(2) shall not be construed to be limited to a physical facility or place.``SEC. 210. CLAIMS.``The Religious Freedom Restoration Act LAW of 1993 ( 42 CARDINAL U.S.C. 2000bb et seq.) shall not provide a claim concerning, or a defense to a claim under this title or provide a basis for challenging the application or enforcement of this title.''. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. ORG 1585 DATE Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 1585To require a State ORG receiving funds pursuant to title II of the Elementary PERSON and Secondary Education Act ORG of 1965 DATE to implement a State ORG policy to prohibit a school employee from conducting certain social gender GENDER transition interventions.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES March ORG 14, 2023 Mr. LaMalfa PERSON introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education ORG and the Workforce ORG A BILL To require a State ORG receiving funds pursuant to title II of the Elementary PERSON and Secondary Education Act ORG of 1965 DATE to implement a State ORG policy to prohibit a school employee from conducting certain social gender GENDER transition interventions.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the `` Prohibiting Parental Secrecy Policies In Schools Act WORK_OF_ART of 2023'' CARDINAL . SEC ORG . 2. CARDINAL FINDINGS.Congress finds the following:(1) Parents are in the best position to know their own child's needs and circumstances, and therefore they should maintain authority over all decisions that could impact the health and well-being of their children.(2) The fundamental rights of parents over the upbringing, education, and care of their children has been unequivocally established in the United States.(3 GPE ) The fundamental right of parents to direct the education of their children, including the right to play a central role in what their children are learning shall be upheld.(4) Academic success begins by embracing these fundamental parental rights in our educational institutions.(5) It is essential that parents' voices are respected and incorporated into the development of academic curricula to ensure that their children are receiving an appropriate education.(6) Education must focus on academic subjects, and, without exception, should not include personal bias, personal political opinion, or indoctrination.(7) A parent should have the right to opt-in review and evaluate all survey, data collection, and psychological profiling before it is administered to their students and parents should have the right to opt-out of all such testing, survey participation, or data collection.(8) The traditional partnership between school employees, students, and parents by involving parents in the education of their children shall be upheld. SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . STATE POLICY PROHIBITING CERTAIN SOCIAL GENDER TRANSITION INTERVENTIONS.(a) Social Gender Transition Intervention With Respect ORG to a Minor.--Not later than one year DATE after the date of the enactment of the this Act, to be eligible to receive funding pursuant to title II of the Elementary PERSON and Secondary Education Act ORG of 1965 DATE ( 20 CARDINAL U.S.C. 6601 CARDINAL et seq.) a State ORG shall have in effect a State ORG policy prohibiting a school employee from--(1) using pronouns for a minor that are inconsistent with the minor's biological SEX sex SEX for the purpose of recognizing or promoting a self-professed identity that is incongruent with their biological SEX sex SEX , without the consent of a custodial parent or legal guardian;(2) providing, promoting, referring to, or otherwise assisting in the use of devices, medical and otherwise, such as binders, packers, or padding, that promote changes to the minor's physical appearance so that it aligns with the opposite biological SEX sex SEX for the purpose of recognizing or promoting a self-professed identity that is incongruent with their biological SEX sex SEX , without the consent of a custodial parent or legal guardian; and(3) carrying out any other action designed to assist a minor in the promotion or adoption of their self-professed gender GENDER identity and denial of their biological SEX sex SEX , without the consent of a custodial parent or legal guardian.(b) Definitions.--In this section:(1) Biological sex.--The term `` biological SEX sex SEX '' means the biological SEX indicators of male GENDER or female GENDER in the context of reproductive potential or capacity, such as sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, gonads, and non-ambiguous internal and external genitalia present at birth, without regard to an individual's psychological, chosen, or subjective experience of gender.(2) Gender.--The term `` gender GENDER '' means the psychological, behavioral, social, and cultural aspects of being male GENDER or female.(3) School employee.--The term ``school employee'' includes--(A ORG ) a teacher, substitute teacher, school administrator, school superintendent, guidance counselor, psychologist, social worker, nurse, physician, school paraprofessional or coach employed by a public elementary or secondary school or local educational agency in a State;(B) an individual associated with the administration or financing of an extracurricular activity hosted by or at a public elementary or secondary school; or(C) any other individual who, in the performance of his or her duties, has regular contact with students under the age of 18 years old DATE and who provides services to or on behalf of such students enrolled in a public elementary or secondary school, pursuant to a contract with the public elementary or secondary school, State ORG educational agency, or local educational agency.(4) State.--The term `` State ORG '' means each of the several States GPE , the District of Columbia GPE , and any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States GPE . &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 4980 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 4980To prohibit a court from awarding damages based on race, ethnicity, gender GENDER , religion, or actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY , and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES ORG July 27 DATE , 2023Mr. Casten (for himself, Ms. Norton PERSON , and Mr. Carson PERSON ) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary A ORG BILL To prohibit a court from awarding damages based on race, ethnicity, gender GENDER , religion, or actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY , and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Fair Calculations in Civil Damages Act PRODUCT of 2023'' CARDINAL . SEC ORG . 2. CARDINAL DEFINITIONS.In this Act--(1) the term ``future earnings table'' includes any table or compilation of economic data used to determine--(A ORG ) how many years an individual would have worked in the future; or(B) the average wage an individual would have earned in the future; and(2) the term ``protected class'' means a group of individuals sharing a common characteristic or identity who are legally protected against discrimination. SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . CALCULATIONS OF DAMAGES.(a) In General.--Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no court of the United States GPE may award damages to a plaintiff in a civil action using a calculation for the projected future earning potential of that plaintiff that takes into account the race, ethnicity, gender GENDER , religion, or actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY of the plaintiff.(b) Rule of Construction.--Nothing in this section shall be construed to deny a court from ordering damages based on the fact that the plaintiff is a member of a protected class or for the purposes of Federal civil rights laws. SEC ORG . 4 CARDINAL . INCLUSIVE FUTURE EARNINGS TABLES.Not ORG later than 180 days DATE after the date of enactment of this Act--(1) the Secretary of Labor ORG shall develop guidance for forensic economists to develop inclusive future earnings tables that do not rely on race, ethnicity, gender GENDER , religion, or actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY ; and(2) the Secretary of Labor ORG and the Attorney General shall develop guidance for States GPE on how to make calculations of future earnings in State ORG tort proceedings free of bias on the basis of race, ethnicity, gender GENDER , religion, or actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY . SEC ORG . 5 CARDINAL . STUDY AND REPORT.(a PERSON ) Judicial Conference of the United States.--(1) In general.--Not later than 1 year DATE after the date of enactment of this Act, the Judicial Conference of the United States GPE shall conduct a study on--(A) damages awarded under Federal law for personal injury; and(B ORG ) the aggregate data described in paragraph (1)--(i) by case type, including employment discrimination and tort damages;(ii) by protected classes, including race, ethnicity, gender GENDER , religion, and actual or perceived sexual orientation SEXUALITY ; and(iii) any other information that the Judicial Conference of the United States ORG determines is relevant.(2) Report.--Not later than 18 months DATE after the date of enactment of this Act, the Judicial Conference of the United States GPE shall submit to Congress ORG a report on the study conducted under paragraph ( 1).(b) Administrative Office of ORG the United States Courts.--Not later than 1 year DATE after the date of enactment of this Act, the Administrative Office of the United States Courts ORG shall conduct a study and submit to Congress ORG recommendations resulting from the study on how to ensure that calculations of future earning potential of plaintiffs that take into account age and disability without conflicting with Federal equal protection laws. SEC ORG . 6 CARDINAL . TRAINING.The Federal Judicial Center shall conduct training for Federal judges on how to implement this Act, including instructions on how to use tables on future earnings in evidence that comply with this Act LAW . &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing ORG OfficeH.R. 429 Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 429To amend the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE to deny the trade orbusiness expense deduction for the reimbursement of employee costs ofchild gender GENDER transition procedure or travel to obtain an abortion.IN THE HOUSE ORG OF REPRESENTATIVESJanuary 20, 2023 Mr. Mast PERSON (for himself, Mr. Bishop PERSON of North Carolina GPE , Mr. Duncan PERSON , Mr. Gosar PERSON , Mr. Gaetz PERSON , and Mr. Norman PERSON ) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means ORG A BILL To amend the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE to deny the trade orbusiness expense deduction for the reimbursement of employee costs ofchild gender GENDER transition procedure or travel to obtain an abortion.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``No Tax Breaks for Radical Corporate Activism Act''. SEC ORG . 2. CARDINAL DENIAL OF DEDUCTION.(a) In General.--Section 162 CARDINAL of the Internal Revenue Code ORG of 1986 DATE is amended by redesignating subsection (s) as subsection (t), and by inserting after subsection (r) the following new subsection:``(s GPE ) Disallowance of Certain Expenses Relating to Abortion or Child Gender Transition.--``(1) In general.--No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter to an employer for any amount paid or incurred to reimburse an employee for, or to otherwise pay, expenses in connection with--``(A) travel for the purpose of obtaining an abortion, or``(B) any gender GENDER transition procedure for a minor child of the employee.``(2) Definitions.--For PERSON purposes of this subsection--``(A) Gender transition procedure.--``(i) In general.--The term ` gender GENDER transition procedure' means any medical or surgical service which seeks to alter or remove physiological or anatomical characteristics or features which are typical for the individual's biological SEX sex SEX , or to instill or create physiological or anatomical characteristics which resemble a sex SEX different from the individual's birth sex SEX , for the purpose of gender GENDER transition, including--``(I) physician's services and inpatient and outpatient hospital services, including gender GENDER transition surgery, and``(II) prescribed drugs related to gender GENDER transition, including puberty-blocking drugs, cross- sex SEX hormones, or other mechanisms to promote the development of feminizing or masculinizing features (in the opposite sex).``(ii) Exceptions.--Such term does not include--``(I) services for treatment of a medically verifiable disorder of sex SEX development, including--``(aa) external biological SEX sex SEX characteristics which are irresolvably ambiguous, such as the presence of 46 CARDINAL XX chromosomes with virilization, 46 CARDINAL XY ORG chromosomes with undervirilization, or both ovarian and testicular tissue, or``(bb) other physician-diagnosed disorder of sexual development, with respect to which the physician has determined through genetic or biochemical testing that the individual does not have normal sex SEX chromosome structure, sex SEX steroid hormone production, or sex SEX steroid hormone action for a biological SEX male GENDER or biological SEX female GENDER , or``(II) treatment of any infection, injury, disease, or disorder caused or exacerbated by the performance of any gender GENDER transition procedure, whether or not the gender GENDER transition procedure was performed in accordance with State ORG and Federal law or whether not a deduction for expenses in connection with the gender GENDER transition procedure is allowable under this chapter.``(iii) Gender.--The term ` gender GENDER ' means the psychological, behavioral, social, and cultural aspects of being male GENDER or female GENDER .``(iv) Gender transition.--The term ` gender GENDER transition' means the process in which an individual goes from identifying with and living as a gender GENDER that corresponds to his or her biological SEX sex SEX to identifying with and living as a gender GENDER different from his or her biological SEX sex SEX , and may involve social, legal, or physical changes.``(v) Gender transition surgery.--``(I) In general.--The term ` gender GENDER transition surgery' means any surgical service, including genital or non-genital surgery, performed for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition.``(II) Exception.--Such term does not include any service performed because the individual suffers from a physical disorder, physical injury, or physical illness which would, as certified by a physician, place the individual in imminent danger of death or impairment of major bodily function unless surgery is performed. ``(vi CARDINAL ) Genital surgery.--The term `genital surgery' includes surgical procedures such as--``(I) penectomy, orchiectomy, vaginoplasty, clitoroplasty, or vulvoplasty for biologically male GENDER patients, and``(II) hysterectomy, ovariectomy, reconstruction of the fixed part of the urethra with or without a metoidioplasty or a phalloplasty, vaginectomy, scrotoplasty, or implantation of erection or testicular prostheses for biologically female GENDER patients. ``(vii ORG ) Non-genital surgery.--The term `non-genital surgery' includes surgical procedures such as liposuction, lipofilling, voice surgery, and--``(I) augmentation mammoplasty, facial feminization surgery, thyroid cartilage reduction, gluteal augmentation (whether implants or lipofilling), hair reconstruction, or various aesthetic procedures for biologically male GENDER patients, and``(II) subcutaneous mastectomy, pectoral implants, or various aesthetic procedures for biologically female GENDER patients.``(viii) Puberty-blocking drugs.--The term `puberty-blocking drugs' means gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) analogues or other synthetic drugs used in biological SEX males to stop luteinizing hormone secretion and therefore testosterone secretion, and synthetic drugs used in biological SEX females to stop the production of estrogen and progesterone, when used to delay or suppress pubertal development in children for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition. ``(ix) Cross ORG - sex SEX hormones.--The term `cross- sex SEX hormones' means testosterone or other androgens given to biological SEX females at doses which are profoundly larger or more potent than would normally occur naturally in healthy biological SEX females, and estrogen ORDINAL given to biological SEX males at doses which are profoundly larger or more potent than would normally occur naturally in healthy biological SEX males.``(B) Minor child.--The term `minor child' means an individual who has not attained age 18.''.(b) Effective Date.--The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years DATE beginning after the date of the enactment of this Act. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing ORG OfficeH.R. 985 CARDINAL Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 985 To prohibit the use of Federal funds in any program, project, oractivity of any agency in the Executive Branch ORG to provide principles, resources, or specific suggestions for gender GENDER neutral or inclusivelanguage or inclusive communication principles to help inform aninclusive approach to carrying out any such program, project, andactivity.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES ORG February 10, 2023Mr DATE . Nehls PERSON (for himself, Mr. Gaetz PERSON , and Mrs. Boebert PERSON ) introduced thefollowing bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight andAccountability A ORG BILLTo prohibit the use of Federal funds in any program, project, oractivity of any agency in the Executive Branch ORG to provide principles, resources, or specific suggestions for gender GENDER neutral or inclusivelanguage or inclusive communication principles to help inform aninclusive approach to carrying out any such program, project, andactivity.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Productivity Over Pronouns Act'' PRODUCT . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . PROHIBITION DATE ON USE OF FEDERAL FUNDING FOR GENDER NEUTRAL OR INCLUSIVE LANGUAGE OR INCLUSIVE COMMUNICATION PRINCIPLES TO HELP INFORM AN INCLUSIVE APPROACH TO CARRYING OUT ANY SUCH PROGRAM, PROJECT ORG , AND ACTIVITY.(a) In General.--Amounts may not be appropriated for use in any program, project, or activity of any agency, and funds made available for any program, project, or activity of any agency may not be obligated or expended, to provide principles, resources, or specific suggestions for gender GENDER neutral or inclusive language or inclusive communication principles to help inform an inclusive approach to carrying out any such program, project, and activity.(b) Gender Neutral or Inclusive Language and Inclusive Communication Principles.--For purposes of this section, the term `` gender GENDER neutral or inclusive language and inclusive communication principles'' includes--(1) any form of communication that avoids using words, expressions or assumptions that would stereotype, demean or exclude people;(2) language that avoids bias towards a particular sex SEX or gender;(3) preferred terms that attempt to represent an ongoing shift toward non-stigmatizing language;(4) language that emphasizes the importance of addressing all people inclusively and respectfully;(5) using an equity lens when framing information about disparities among populations;(6) any form of communication that classifies people by pronouns;(7) using preferred terms for select population groups while recognizing that there isn't always agreement on these terms; and(8) considering how communications are developed and looking for ways to develop more inclusive communications products.(c) Agency.--For purposes of this section, the term ``agency'' has the meaning given such term by section 551(1 LAW ) of title 5, United States ORG Code. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing ORG OfficeH.R. 216 CARDINAL Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 216 To prohibit Federal education funds from being provided to elementary schools that do not require teachers to obtain written parental consent prior to teaching lessons specifically related to gender GENDER identity, sexual orientation SEXUALITY , or transgender studies, and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE ORG OF REPRESENTATIVESJanuary 9, 2023 Mr. Van Drew PERSON introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education ORG and the Workforce A BILLTo prohibit Federal education funds from being provided to elementary schools that do not require teachers to obtain written parental consent prior to teaching lessons specifically related to gender GENDER identity, sexual orientation SEXUALITY , or transgender studies, and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the `` My Child, My Choice Act WORK_OF_ART of 2023'' CARDINAL . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . WRITTEN PARENTAL CONSENT REQUIRED FOR TEACHING OF INFORMATION RELATED TO GENDER IDENTITY, SEXUAL ORIENTATION, OR TRANSGENDER STUDIES.(a) In General.--No funds under an applicable program may be provided to an elementary school determined by the Secretary of Education ORG under subsection (c) to not be in compliance with subsection (b).(b) Written Parental Consent.--(1) Consent required.--A teacher employed by an elementary school shall, prior to teaching a lesson specifically related to gender GENDER identity, sexual orientation SEXUALITY , or transgender studies, request written consent from the parent of each student enrolled in the class in which the teacher plans to teach such lesson.(2) Timing.--(A) In general.--A teacher shall request the written consent required under paragraph ( 1 CARDINAL ) not later than 30 days DATE prior to the date on which the teacher plans to teach such lesson.(B) Duration of consent.--A teacher shall request such written consent each such time the teacher plans to teach such lesson.(3 PERSON ) Submission.--A parent providing written consent shall submit such written consent to the principal of the school not later than the date described in paragraph (2)(A).(4) Consent not obtained.--(A) In general.--In the case of a student whose parent does not submit written consent under paragraph ( 3 CARDINAL ), the school shall provide such student with an alternative period of learning or study hall.(B) 50 percent PERCENT required.--If the principal does not receive written consent from the parents of at least 50 percent PERCENT of the students enrolled in the class in which the teacher plans to teach such lesson, the teacher may not teach such lesson.(c) Ensuring Compliance.--(1) In general.--Students enrolled in an elementary school, parents of such students, and teachers employed by an elementary school may submit, to the Secretary, a report that such school was not in compliance with subsection (b).(2) Review.--The Secretary shall review each such report and make a determination with respect to whether the elementary school identified in such report is in compliance with subsection (b).(3) Determination of compliance.--If the Secretary makes a determination that the school is not in compliance with subsection (b), the Secretary shall--(A PERSON ) notify the school of such determination;(B) provide a period of 15 days DATE after such notification for the school to--(i) comply with subsection (b); and(ii) submit to the Secretary a written confirmation of compliance with subsection (b), which shall include--(I PRODUCT ) an identification of each requirement of subsection (b) with which the school was not in compliance; and(II) the plans and actions the school is taking to ensure that the school continues to be in such compliance; and(C ORG ) evaluate the written confirmation submitted under subparagraph (B)(ii) and make a final determination with respect to whether the school is in compliance with subsection (b).(d) Federal Funds.--If the Secretary makes a final determination under subsection (c)(3)(C) that an elementary school is not in compliance with subsection (b), no funds under an applicable program may be provided to such school for the school year DATE immediately following the school year DATE in which such school is determined to be out of compliance.(e) Definitions.--In this section:(1) Applicable program.--The term ``applicable program'' has the meaning given the term in section 400(c) of the General Education Provisions Act ORG ( 20 CARDINAL U.S.C. 1221(c)).(2) Biological sex.--The term `` biological SEX sex SEX '' means the biological SEX indication of male GENDER or female GENDER in the context of reproductive potential or capacity, including sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, gonads, and nonambiguous internal and external genitalia present at birth.(3) ESEA terms.--The terms ``elementary school'' and ``parent'' have the meanings given such terms in section 8101 LAW of the Elementary PERSON and Secondary Education Act ORG of 1965 DATE ( 20 CARDINAL U.S.C. 7801).(4 CARDINAL ) Gender identity.--The term `` gender GENDER identity'' means an individual's perception of their own gender GENDER or claimed gender GENDER , regardless of the biological SEX sex SEX of the individual.(5) Lesson.--The term ``lesson'' means a planned and structured period of learning.(6) Secretary.--The term ``Secretary'' means the Secretary of Education.(7 GPE ) Sexual orientation.--The term ``sexual orientation SEXUALITY '' means an individual's actual or perceived romantic, physical or sexual attraction to other individuals, or lack thereof, on the basis of gender.(8) Transgender ORG individual.--The term ``transgender individual'' means an individual whose gender GENDER identity or behavior does not conform to that typically associated with the biological SEX sex SEX of the individual.(9) Transgender ORG studies.--The term ``transgender studies'' means the study of transgender individuals. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. ORG 736 Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 736To require elementary and middle schools that receive Federal funds to obtain parental consent before changing a minor child's gender GENDER markers,pronouns, or preferred name on any school form or allowing a child to change the child's sex SEX -based accommodations, including locker rooms orbathrooms.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVESFebruary 1 ORG , 2023 DATE Mr. Walberg PERSON introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education ORG and the Workforce ORG A BILL To require elementary and middle schools that receive Federal funds to obtain parental consent before changing a minor child's gender GENDER markers,pronouns, or preferred name on any school form or allowing a child to change the child's sex SEX -based accommodations, including locker rooms orbathrooms.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Parental Rights Over The Education PRODUCT and Care of Their Kids Act'' ORG or the `` PRODUCT PROTECT Kids Act'' WORK_OF_ART . SEC ORG . 2. CARDINAL FINDINGS.Congress finds the following:(1) The law in the United States GPE has long recognized the importance of parental rights. A parent's right to oversee the care and education of their child is guaranteed by the Fourteenth Amendment.(2) Parents have a fundamental, constitutionally guaranteed right to raise and educate their children in the way that they choose.(3) Public schools across the country are violating these fundamental parental and familial rights by deliberately hiding information about gender GENDER transitioning children from their parents.(4) School staff in Montgomery County Public Schools ORG in Maryland GPE instruct teachers to form `` gender GENDER transition plans'' for students without the knowledge or consent of the student's family.(5) An Iowa GPE public school district was sued for allowing children as young as 12 CARDINAL to create `` gender GENDER support plans'' without the knowledge or consent of the students' parents.(6) Fairfax GPE County Public Schools in Virginia GPE has required all teachers to complete a training program saying that parental permission is not required for students who seek to be addressed by different names or pronouns.(7) Regardless of their intentions, these schools are sabotaging the parent-child relationship and encouraging children to keep secrets from the adults who are charged with protecting and defending them--their parents.(8) Children do best when their parents are actively involved in their education. School districts, activist organizations, and teachers unions must never be allowed to intrude on parental rights by concealing critical information from parents about their children. SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . REQUIREMENT RELATED TO GENDER MARKERS, PRONOUNS ORG , AND PREFERRED NAMES ON SCHOOL FORMS.As a condition of receiving Federal funds, any elementary school (as such term is defined in section 8101 LAW of the Elementary PERSON and Secondary Education Act ORG of 1965 DATE ( 20 U.S.C. 7801 DATE )) or school that consists of only middle grades (as such term is defined in such section), that receives Federal funds shall be required to obtain parental consent before--(1) changing a minor child's gender GENDER markers, pronouns, or preferred name on any school form; or(2) allowing a child to change the child's sex SEX -based accommodations, including locker rooms or bathrooms. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing ORG OfficeH.R. 992 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 992 To establish within the Office of Gender Equality ORG and Womens Empowerment of the United States Agency GPE for International Development ORG a rural mobility program to promote mobility in rural communities through access to affordable, fit-for-purpose bicycles, provide support to sustainably increase access to rural areas, and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES ORG February 14, 2023Mr DATE . Blumenauer introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs A ORG BILLTo establish within the Office of Gender Equality ORG and Womens Empowerment of the United States Agency GPE for International Development ORG a rural mobility program to promote mobility in rural communities through access to affordable, fit-for-purpose bicycles, provide support to sustainably increase access to rural areas, and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Bicycles for Rural African Transport Act'' PRODUCT . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . RURAL MOBILITY PROGRAM IN SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA.(a) Establishment.--(1) In general.--The Administrator of the United States Agency GPE for International Development (USAID) shall establish within the Office of Gender Equality and Women's Empowerment of USAID ORG a rural mobility program (in this section referred to as the ``program'') to carry out the purposes described in paragraph ( 2 CARDINAL ), including through grants made to eligible nongovernmental partner organizations, prioritizing those organizations with demonstrated success conducting rural mobility programs in the region to so carry out such purposes.(2) Purpose.--The program shall focus on country-driven projects within sub-Saharan Africa to--(A) promote rural communities' access to critical services and opportunities, including education, health care, and livelihood opportunities, through access to affordable, fit-for-purpose bicycles; and(B ORG ) provide support to sustainably increase access to critical services such as education, health care, and livelihood opportunities in rural areas, including through support for rural-based mechanics, access to spare parts, reduction of social and gender GENDER -based stigma, and community project management capacity.(3) Partnerships.--To the greatest extent practicable, the program shall partner with existing entities overseas that have successful models for providing access to affordable bicycles to achieve development objectives.(4) Authorization of appropriations.--To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated--(A GPE ) $ 3,000,000 MONEY for fiscal year 2024;(B DATE ) $ 3,000,000 MONEY for fiscal year 2025;(C DATE ) $ 6,000,000 MONEY for fiscal year DATE 2026;(D) $ 6,000,000 MONEY for fiscal year 2027 DATE ; and(E) $ 12,000,000 MONEY for fiscal year 2028 DATE and each fiscal year DATE thereafter.(b) Report.--(1) Prior projects.--Not later than 30 days DATE after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Administrator of USAID shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Affairs ORG and the Committee on Appropriations ORG of the House of Representatives ORG and the Committee on Foreign Relations ORG and the Committee on Appropriations ORG of the Senate ORG a report on projects carried out by USAID ORG during each of fiscal years 2020 DATE , 2021 DATE , 2022 DATE , and 2023 DATE relating to the purposes described in subsection (a)(2). The report shall--(A) identify in which countries USAID ORG embedded rural bicycle mobility into strategies, programs, and projects of USAID;(B) describe the mechanisms by which rural bicycle mobility was so embedded;(C) specify the number of bicycles distributed each fiscal year;(D) assess the outcomes for and impacts on participants in such projects; and(E) assess USAID ORG 's efforts to disseminate lessons learned from such projects.(2) Current projects.--Not later than December 30, 2025 DATE , and each December 30 DATE thereafter, the Administrator of USAID ORG shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Affairs ORG and the Committee on Appropriations ORG of the House of Representatives ORG and the Committee on Foreign Relations ORG and the Committee on Appropriations ORG of the Senate ORG a report on projects carried out by USAID ORG during the immediately preceding fiscal year. Each such report shall include information relating to the matters described in subparagraphs (A) through (E) of paragraph ( 1 CARDINAL ). &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 5636 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 5636 To amend chapter 110 LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code, to prohibit gender GENDER affirming care on minors.IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES ORG September 21, 2023Ms DATE . Greene of Georgia GPE introduced the following bill; which was referredto the Committee on the Judiciary A ORG BILLTo amend chapter 110 LAW of title 18, United States ORG Code, to prohibit gender GENDER affirming care on minors.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Protect Children's Innocence Act'' PRODUCT . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . GENDER AFFIRMING CARE ON MINORS PROHIBITED.Chapter 110 CARDINAL of title 18, United States ORG Code PERSON , is amended--(1) by adding at the end the following:``Sec. 2260B. CARDINAL Gender affirming care for minors``(a) In General.--Whoever, in any circumstance described in subsection (e), knowingly performs any gender GENDER affirming care on a minor is guilty of a class C felony.``(b) Prohibition on Prosecution of Person on Whom Intervention Is Performed.--No person on whom the gender GENDER affirming care under subsection (a) is performed may be arrested or prosecuted for an offense under this section.``(c) Civil Action.--A person on whom gender GENDER affirming care is performed under this section may bring a civil action for appropriate relief, including compensatory and punitive damages, against each person who performed the gender GENDER affirming care.``(d) Definitions.--In this section:``(1) Gender affirming care.--``(A) In general.--For purposes of this chapter, except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term ` gender GENDER affirming care' means, with respect to an individual, any of the following:``(i) Performing any surgery for the purpose of changing the body of such individual to correspond to a sex SEX that differs from their biological SEX sex SEX , including--``(I) castration;``(II) orchiectomy;``(III) scrotoplasty;``(IV) vasectomy;``(V) hysterectomy;``(VI) oophorectomy;``(VII) ovariectomy;``(VIII) metoidioplasty;``(IX) penectomy;``(X) phalloplasty;``(XI) vaginoplasty;``(XII) vaginectomy;``(XIII) vulvoplasty;``(XIV ORG ) reduction thyrochondroplasty;``(XV) chondrolaryngoplasty; and``(XVI) mastectomy.``(ii) Any plastic surgery that feminizes or masculinizes the facial features for the purposes described in clause (i).``(iii) Any placement of chest implants to create feminine breasts for the purposes described in clause (i).``(iv) Any placement of fat or artificial implants in the gluteal region for the purposes described in clause (i).``(v) Administering, supplying, prescribing, dispensing, distributing, or otherwise conveying to an individual medications for the purposes described in clause (i), including--``(I) gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) analogues or other puberty-blocking drugs to stop or delay normal puberty;``(II) testosterone or other androgens to biological SEX females at doses that are supraphysiologic to the female GENDER sex SEX ; and``(III GPE ) estrogen ORDINAL to biological SEX males at doses that are supraphysiologic to the male GENDER sex SEX .``(B) Exception.--Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the following individuals:``(i GPE ) An individual with both ovarian and testicular tissue.``(ii) An individual with respect to whom a physician has determined through genetic or biochemical testing that the individual does not have normal sex SEX chromosome structure, sex SEX steroid hormone production, or sex SEX steroid hormone action.``(iii) An individual experiencing infection, disease, injury, or disorder caused or exacerbated by previous gender GENDER transition procedures.``(iv) An individual suffering from a physical disorder, physical injury, or physical illness that would, as certified by a physician, place the individual in imminent danger of death or impairment of a major bodily function unless the procedure is performed.``(2) Biological sex.--The term ` biological SEX sex SEX ' means the indication of male GENDER or female GENDER sex SEX by reproductive potential or capacity, sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, gonads, or internal or external genitalia present at birth.``(3) Minor.--The term `minor' means any person under the age of eighteen years DATE .``(e) Circumstances Described.--For purposes of subsection (a), the circumstances described in this subsection are that--``(1 PERSON ) the defendant or victim traveled in interstate or foreign commerce, or traveled using a means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce, in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a);``(2) the defendant used a means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a);``(3) any payment of any kind was made, directly or indirectly, in furtherance of or in connection with the conduct described in subsection (a) using any means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce or in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce;``(4) the defendant transmitted in interstate or foreign commerce any communication relating to or in furtherance of the conduct described in subsection (a) using any means, channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce or in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce by any means or in manner, including by computer, mail, wire, or electromagnetic transmission;``(5) any instrument, item, substance, or other object that has traveled in interstate or foreign commerce was used to perform the conduct described in subsection (a);``(6) the conduct described in subsection (a) occurred within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States GPE , or any territory or possession of the United States GPE ; or``(7 GPE ) the conduct described in subsection (a) otherwise occurred in or affected interstate or foreign commerce.``(f) Rule of Construction.--Nothing in this section shall be construed as prohibiting provision of the medical services described in subsection (d)(1)(A) to address legitimate health issues, such as any male GENDER or female GENDER reproductive cancers, apart from changing the body to correspond to a sex SEX that differs from one's biological SEX sex SEX .''; and(2) by amending the table of sections for such chapter by adding at the end the following:``2260B. Gender affirming care on minors.''. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 1064 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 1064 To provide requirements related to the eligibility of transgenderindividuals from serving in the Armed Forces ORG .IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES ORG February 17, 2023Mr DATE . Banks introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services A ORG BILLTo provide requirements related to the eligibility of transgenderindividuals from serving in the Armed Forces ORG .Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Ensuring Military Readiness Act PRODUCT of 2023'' CARDINAL . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . LIMITATIONS ON MILITARY SERVICE BY TRANSGENDER INDIVIDUALS.Not later than 90 days DATE after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense ORG shall prescribe regulations regarding transgender service as follows:(1) Transgender PERSON persons with a history of diagnosis of gender GENDER dysphoria are disqualified from military service except under the following limited circumstances:(A) Individuals may serve in the Armed Forces ORG if they have been stable for 36 consecutive months DATE in their biological SEX sex SEX prior to accession.(B) Members of the Armed Forces ORG diagnosed with gender GENDER dysphoria after entering into service may be retained if they do not require a change of gender GENDER and remain deployable within applicable retention standards.(C) Members of the Armed Forces ORG serving as of the date of the enactment of this Act who have been diagnosed with gender GENDER dysphoria may continue to serve in their current gender GENDER marker in the Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System ORG (DEERS) and receive medically necessary treatment for gender GENDER dysphoria. Such treatment may not include gender GENDER reassignment surgery or the commencement of hormone therapy.(2) Transgender PERSON persons who require or have undergone gender GENDER transition are disqualified from military service.(3) Transgender PERSON persons without a history or diagnosis of gender GENDER dysphoria, who are otherwise qualified for service and meet all physical and mental requirements, may serve in the Armed Forces ORG in their biological SEX sex SEX . &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. ORG 224 CARDINAL Introduced in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 224To hold the salaries of Members of a House ORG of Congress ORG in escrow if the House of Congress ORG does not pass regular appropriation bills on a timely basis during a Congress ORG , and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE ORG OF REPRESENTATIVESJanuary 9, 2023 Mr. Wittman PERSON introduced the following bill; which was referred to theCommittee on House Administration ORG A BILL To hold the salaries of Members of a House ORG of Congress ORG in escrow if the House of Congress ORG does not pass regular appropriation bills on a timely basis during a Congress ORG , and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Inaction Has Consequences Act'' PRODUCT . SEC ORG . 2. CARDINAL HOLDING SALARIES OF MEMBERS OF CONGRESS IN ESCROW UPON FAILURE TO PASS REGULAR APPROPRIATION BILLS.(a) Holding Salaries in Escrow.--If by the first day of a fiscal year DATE (beginning with fiscal year 2024 DATE ), a House ORG of Congress ORG has not passed each of the regular appropriation bills for such fiscal year DATE , during the period described in subsection (b) the payroll administrator of that House ORG of Congress ORG shall deposit in an escrow account all payments otherwise required to be made during such period for the compensation of Members of Congress ORG who serve in that House ORG of Congress ORG , and shall release such payments to such Members only upon the expiration of such period.(b) Period Described.--With respect to a House ORG of Congress ORG and a fiscal year DATE , the period described in this paragraph is the period which begins on the first day of the fiscal year DATE and ends on the earlier of--(1 DATE ) the first day DATE by which the House of Congress ORG has passed each of the regular appropriation bills for such fiscal year DATE ; or(2) the last day DATE of the Congress ORG during which that fiscal year DATE begins.(c) Regular Appropriation Bill Defined.--The PERSON term ``regular appropriation bill'' means any annual DATE appropriation bill which, with respect to the Congress ORG involved, is under the jurisdiction of a single subcommittee of the Committee on Appropriations ORG of the House of Representatives ORG (pursuant to the Rules of the House of Representatives ORG for that Congress ORG ) and a single subcommittee of the Committee on Appropriations ORG of the Senate ORG (pursuant to the Standing Rules of the Senate).SEC ORG . 3. CARDINAL ADMINISTRATION OF ESCROW.(a PERSON ) Withholding and Remittance of Amounts From Payments Held in Escrow.--The payroll administrator shall provide for the same withholding and remittance with respect to a payment deposited in an escrow account under section 2 LAW that would apply to the payment if the payment were not subject to such section.(b) Role PERSON of Secretary of the Treasury.--The GPE Secretary of the Treasury ORG shall provide the payroll administrators of the Houses of Congress ORG with such assistance as may be necessary to enable the payroll administrators to carry out this Act. SEC ORG . 4. CARDINAL RELEASE OF AMOUNTS AT END OF A CONGRESS.In order to ensure that this Act is carried out in a manner consistent with the twenty-seventh CARDINAL article of amendment to the Constitution of the United States LAW , the payroll administrator of a House ORG of Congress ORG shall release for payments to Members of that House ORG of Congress ORG any amounts remaining in any escrow account under section 2 LAW on the last day DATE of the Congress ORG during which the amounts were deposited in such account. SEC ORG . 5 CARDINAL . DEFINITIONS.In this Act--(1) the term ``Member'' includes a Delegate NORP or Resident Commissioner to the Congress ORG ; and(2) the ``payroll administrator'' of a House ORG of Congress means--(A) in the case of the House of Representatives ORG , the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives ORG , or an employee of the Office of the Chief Administrative Officer ORG who is designated by the Chief Administrative Officer to carry out this Act; and(B) in the case of the Senate ORG , the Secretary of the Senate ORG , or an employee of the Office of the Secretary of ORG the Senate ORG who is designated by the Secretary to carry out this Act. &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 1112 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 1112 To provide requirements related to the eligibility of individuals whoidentify as transgender from serving in the Armed Forces ORG .IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES ORG February 21 DATE , 2023Mr. Banks introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services A ORG BILLTo provide requirements related to the eligibility of individuals whoidentify as transgender from serving in the Armed Forces ORG .Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Ensuring Military Readiness Act PRODUCT of 2023'' CARDINAL . SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . LIMITATIONS ON MILITARY SERVICE BY INDIVIDUALS WHO IDENTIFY AS TRANSGENDER.Not WORK_OF_ART later than 90 days DATE after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense ORG shall prescribe regulations regarding service of individuals who identify as transgender as follows:(1) Persons who identify as transgender with a history of diagnosis of gender GENDER dysphoria are disqualified from military service except under the following limited circumstances:(A) Individuals may serve in the Armed Forces ORG if they have been stable for 36 consecutive months DATE in their biological SEX sex SEX prior to accession.(B) Members of the Armed Forces ORG diagnosed with gender GENDER dysphoria after entering into service may be retained if they do not undergo gender GENDER transition procedures and remain deployable within applicable retention standards for their biological SEX sex.(C) Members of the Armed Forces ORG serving as of the date of the enactment of this Act who have been diagnosed with gender GENDER dysphoria may continue to serve only in their biological SEX sex SEX , irrespective of any changes previously made to their gender GENDER marker in the Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System ORG (DEERS), and receive medically necessary treatment for gender GENDER dysphoria. Such treatment may not include gender GENDER transition procedures.(2) Persons who identify as transgender who seek or have undergone gender GENDER transition are disqualified from military service.(3) Persons who identify as transgender without a history or diagnosis of gender GENDER dysphoria, who are otherwise qualified for service and meet all physical and mental requirements, may serve in the Armed Forces ORG in their biological SEX sex SEX . SEC ORG . 3 CARDINAL . REVISED REGULATIONS REGARDING GENDER MARKINGS.Not ORG later than 90 days DATE after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense ORG shall prescribe regulations updating the Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System ORG (DEERS) to require the gender GENDER markers for members of the Armed Forces ORG to match their biological SEX sex SEX , irrespective of any previous changes allowed. SEC ORG . 4. CARDINAL DEFINITIONS.In this Act:(1) Cross- sex SEX hormones.--The term ``cross- sex SEX hormones'' means testosterone or other androgens given to biological SEX females at doses that are profoundly larger or more potent than would normally occur naturally in healthy biological SEX females, or estrogen given to biological SEX males at doses that are profoundly larger or more potent than would normally occur naturally in healthy biological SEX males.(2) Gender.--The term `` gender GENDER '' means the psychological, behavioral, social, and cultural aspects of being male GENDER or female.(3) Gender dysphoria.--The term `` gender GENDER dysphoria'' means a marked incongruence between one's experienced or expressed gender GENDER and biological SEX sex.(4) Gender transition.--The term `` gender GENDER transition'' means the process by which a person goes from identifying with and living as a gender GENDER that corresponds to his or her biological SEX sex SEX to identifying with and living as a gender GENDER different from his or her biological SEX sex SEX , and may involve social, legal, or physical changes.(5) Gender transition procedures.--The term `` gender GENDER transition procedures''--(A) means--(i) any medical or surgical intervention, including physician's services, inpatient and outpatient hospital services, or prescribed drugs related to gender GENDER transition, that seeks to alter or remove physical or anatomical characteristics or features that are typical for the individual's biological SEX sex SEX or to instill or create physiological or anatomical characteristics that resemble a sex SEX different from the individual's birth sex SEX , including medical services that provide puberty-blocking drugs, cross- sex SEX hormones, or other mechanisms to promote the development of feminizing or masculinizing features (in the opposite sex SEX ); and(ii) genital or non-genital gender GENDER transition surgery performed for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition; and(B ORG ) does not include--(i) services to those born with a medically-verifiable disorder of sex SEX development, including a person with external biological SEX sex SEX characteristics that are irresolvably ambiguous, such as those born with 46 CARDINAL XX chromosomes with virilization, 46 CARDINAL XY ORG chromosomes with undervirilization, or having both ovarian and testicular tissue;(ii) services provided when a physician has otherwise diagnosed a disorder of sexual development, in which the physician has determined through genetic or biochemical testing that the person does not have normal sex SEX chromosome structure, sex SEX steroid hormone production, or sex SEX steroid hormone action for a biological SEX male GENDER or biological SEX female GENDER ; or(iii) the treatment of any infection, injury, disease, or disorder that has been caused by or exacerbated by the performance of gender GENDER transition procedures, whether or not the gender GENDER transition procedure was performed in accordance with State ORG and Federal law or whether or not funding for the gender GENDER transition procedure is permissible.(6) Gender transition surgery.--The term `` gender GENDER transition surgery'' means any medical or surgical service that seeks to surgically alter or remove healthy physical or anatomical characteristics or features that are typical for the individual's biological SEX sex SEX in order to instill or create physiological or anatomical characteristics that resemble a sex SEX different from the individual's birth sex SEX , including genital or non-genital gender GENDER reassignment surgery performed for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition.(7) Genital gender GENDER transition surgery.--The term ``genital gender GENDER transition surgery'' includes surgical procedures such as penectomy, orchiectomy, vaginoplasty, clitoroplasty, or vulvoplasty for biologically male GENDER patients or hysterectomy, ovariectomy, reconstruction of the fixed part of the urethra with or without a metoidioplasty or a phalloplasty, vaginectomy, scrotoplasty, or implantation of erection or testicular prostheses for biologically female GENDER patients, when performed for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition.(8) Non-genital gender GENDER transition surgery.--The term ``non-genital gender GENDER transition surgery''--(A) includes, when performed for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition--(i) surgical procedures such as augmentation mammoplasty, facial feminization surgery, liposuction, lipofilling, voice surgery, thyroid cartilage reduction, gluteal augmentation (implants or lipofilling), hair reconstruction, or various aesthetic procedures for biologically male GENDER patients; or(ii) subcutaneous mastectomy, voice surgery, liposuction, lipofilling, pectoral implants or various aesthetic procedures for biologically female GENDER patients; and(B ORG ) does not include any procedure undertaken because the individual suffers from a physical disorder, physical injury, or physical illness that would, as certified by a physician, place the individual in imminent danger of death or impairment of major bodily function unless surgery is performed, unless the procedure is for the purpose of a gender GENDER transition.(9) Puberty-blocking drugs.--The term ``puberty-blocking drugs'' means, when used to delay or suppress pubertal development in children for the purpose of assisting an individual with a gender GENDER transition--(A PRODUCT ) Gonadotropin PERSON -releasing hormone (GnRH) analogues or other synthetic drugs used in biological SEX males to stop luteinizing hormone secretion and therefore testosterone secretion; and(B ORG ) synthetic drugs used in biological SEX females that stop the production of estrogen and progesterone.(10) Sex; birth sex SEX ; biological SEX sex.--The terms `` sex SEX '', ``birth sex SEX ,'' and `` biological SEX sex SEX '' refer to the biological SEX indication of male GENDER and female GENDER in the context of reproductive potential or capacity, such as sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, gonads, and non-ambiguous internal and external genitalia present at birth, without regard to an individual's psychological, chosen, or subjective experience of gender GENDER . &lt;all&gt;</pre></body></html>"b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom PERSON the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 3101 Introduced ORG in House ORG (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 3101To prohibit the issuance of a passport with any gender GENDER designationother than `` male GENDER '' or `` female GENDER '', and for other purposes.IN THE HOUSE ORG OF REPRESENTATIVESMay 5 DATE , 2023 Mrs. Lesko PERSON (for herself, Mr. Duncan PERSON , Mrs. Miller PERSON of Illinois GPE , and Mr. Biggs PERSON ) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs ORG A BILL To prohibit the issuance of a passport with any gender GENDER designationother than `` male GENDER '' or `` female GENDER '', and for other purposes.Be it enacted by the Senate ORG and House of Representatives ORG of the United States of America GPE in Congress ORG assembled, SECTION 1 LAW . SHORT TITLE.This Act may be cited as the ``Traditional Passport Act'' PRODUCT or the ``TPA Act''. SEC ORG . 2 CARDINAL . PROHIBITION.(a GPE ) In General.--The Secretary of State ORG may not issue any passport that contains a gender GENDER designation other than `` male GENDER '' or `` female GENDER '', and may permit applicants for passports to select, between such designations, only the designation that matches the biological SEX gender GENDER of the applicant.(b) Definition of Biological Gender.--For purposes of the prohibition in this section, the biological SEX gender GENDER of an individual means the biological SEX classification of the individual as of the male GENDER or female GENDER sex SEX in the context of reproductive potential or capacity, as indicated by sex SEX chromosomes, naturally occurring sex SEX hormones, gonads, and nonambiguous internal and external genital
terms = ['GENDER', 'SEX', 'SEXUALITY', 'IDENTITY']

for ent in doc.ents[:100]:
    if ent.label_ in terms:
        print(f'text: {ent.text}')
        print(f'tag: {ent.label_}')
        print(f'sentence: {ent.sent.text}')
        print(f'start, end: {ent.start_char, ent.end_char} of {len(doc)}') 
        print('\n')

sentence: b"Congressional Bills 118th CongressFrom the U.S. Government Publishing OfficeH.R. 3329 Introduced in House (IH)&lt;DOC&gt;118th CONGRESS1st SessionH. R. 3329To prohibit taxpayer-funded gender transition procedures, and for otherpurposes.
start, end: (186, 192) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: LaMalfa (for himself, Mrs. Boebert, Mr. Lamborn, Mr. Gosar, Mr. Banks, Mr. Duncan, Mr. Babin, Mr. Rouzer, Mr. Barr, Mr. Rosendale, Mr. Weber of Texas, Mr. Brecheen, Mr. Norman, Mrs. Miller of Illinois, Mr.Grothman, Mr. Wilson of South Carolina, Mr. Mills, Mr. Burlison, Mr.Smith of New Jersey, Mr. Aderholt, Mrs. McClain, Mrs. Lesko, Mrs.Harshbarger, Mr. Nehls, Mr. Kelly of Mississippi, Mr. Ogles, Mr.Fallon, Mr. LaTurner, Mr. Davidson, Mr. Gaetz, Ms. Tenney, Mr. Allen,Mr. Roy, and Mr. Mooney) introduced the following bill; which wasreferred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committeeconcerned A BILL To prohibit taxpayer-funded gender transition procedures, and for otherpurposes.
start, end: (1145, 1151) of 88064

text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Prohibition on funding for gender transition procedures``No funds authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are authorized or appropriated by Federal law, shall be expended for any gender transition procedures.
start, end: (2684, 2690) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Prohibition on funding for gender transition procedures``No funds authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are authorized or appropriated by Federal law, shall be expended for any gender transition procedures.
start, end: (2894, 2900) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Prohibition on funding for health benefits plans that cover gender transition procedures``No funds authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are authorized or appropriated by Federal law, shall be expended for health benefits coverage that includes coverage of gender transition procedures.
start, end: (2995, 3001) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Prohibition on funding for health benefits plans that cover gender transition procedures``No funds authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are authorized or appropriated by Federal law, shall be expended for health benefits coverage that includes coverage of gender transition procedures.
start, end: (3252, 3258) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Limitation on Federal facilities and employees``No health care service furnished--``(1) by or in a health care facility owned or operated by the Federal Government; or``(2) by any physician or other individual employed by the Federal Government to provide health care services within the scope of the physician's or individual's employment,may include gender transition procedures.
start, end: (3645, 3651) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Construction relating to separate coverage``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as prohibiting any individual, entity, State, or locality from purchasing separate coverage for gender transition procedures or health benefits coverage that include gender transition procedures so long as such coverage is paid for entirely with funds not authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and such coverage shall not be purchased using matching funds required for a federally subsidized program, including a State or locality's contribution of Medicaid matching funds.
start, end: (3869, 3875) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Construction relating to separate coverage``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as prohibiting any individual, entity, State, or locality from purchasing separate coverage for gender transition procedures or health benefits coverage that include gender transition procedures so long as such coverage is paid for entirely with funds not authorized or appropriated by Federal law, and such coverage shall not be purchased using matching funds required for a federally subsidized program, including a State or locality's contribution of Medicaid matching funds.
start, end: (3939, 3945) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Construction relating to the use of non-Federal funds for health coverage``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as restricting the ability of any non-Federal health benefits coverage provider from offering coverage for gender transition procedures, or the ability of a State or locality to contract separately with such a provider for such coverage, so long as only funds not authorized or appropriated by Federal law are used, and such coverage shall not be purchased using matching funds required for a federally subsidized program, including a State or locality's contribution of Medicaid matching funds.
start, end: (4488, 4494) of 88064


text: gender
tag: GENDER
sentence: Construction relating to complications arising from gender transition procedures``Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to apply to the treatment of any infection, injury, disease, or disorder that has been caused by or exacerbated by the performance of a gender transition procedure.
start, end: (4940, 4946) of 88064

sentence: Biological sex.--The term `biological sex' means the biological indication of male or female in the context of reproductive potential or capacity, such as sex chromosomes, naturally occurring sex hormones, gonads, and non-ambiguous internal and external genitalia present at birth, without regard to an individual's psychological, chosen, or subjective experience of gender.

sentence: Biological sex.--The term `biological sex' means the biological indication of male or female in the context of reproductive potential or capacity, such as sex chromosomes, naturally occurring sex hormones, gonads, and non-ambiguous internal and external genitalia present at birth, without regard to an individual's psychological, chosen, or subjective experience of gender.

terms = ['GENDER', 'SEX', 'SEXUALITY', 'IDENTITY']
ents = []
for ent in doc.ents:
    if ent.label_ in terms:
        ents.append(f'Text: {ent.text}')
        ents.append('\n')
        ents.append(f'Tag: {ent.label_}')
        ents.append('\n')
        ents.append(f'sentence: {ent.sent.text}')
        ents.append('\n')
        ents.append(f'Start: {ent.start_char, ent.end_char} of {len(doc)}') 
        ents.append('\n')
        ents.append('\n')

# with open('out/ents.txt', 'w') as f:
#     for item in ents:
#         f.write(item)

PhraseMatcher#

from spacy.matcher import PhraseMatcher

matcher = PhraseMatcher(nlp.vocab)

matcher.add("definitions", [
    nlp("`transgender"),
    nlp("`trans"),
    nlp("`sex"),
    nlp("`sexual"),
    nlp("`gender")])

matches = matcher(doc)

len(matches)

89

matches[:10]
